Turn decision with pocket eights on 6h-Kc-Tc-2s. Elon raised the flop to 300 but checked the turn, which suggests either weakness or a hand reluctant to build the pot further. The deuce is a blank that doesn't help his likely range meaningfully. My pair of eights is a solid hand against a wide SB range, and the checking action presents an opportunity to apply pressure and potentially win the pot now. A bet of 500 chips (5BB) is appropriately sized—large enough to exert real pressure on weaker hands or bluffs, while not overcommitting my healthy 14,490 stack relative to the 1,200 pot. This bet also builds the pot favorably if Elon calls with a worse hand like an unpaired broadway holding. Given Elon's propensity to check back moderate holdings on turns, I expect to win some percentage of pots immediately while still getting called by hands that are either ahead (made pairs/sets) or have reasonable equity (draws). This is the optimal balance of aggression and stack preservation.

Facing a turn bet of 500 into 1700 after checking OOP on a K-T-6-2 board with two clubs. AhJc is ace-high with no pair, draw, or significant showdown value against Claude's range, which includes value hands like Kx, Tx, sets, two-pair, straights (QJ, 98), and combo draws that called the flop c-bet and now barrel. The preflop raise and flop c-bet represented strength, but checking turn showed weakness, polarizing his betting range toward value. Pot odds are about 3:1 (500 to win 2200), but equity realization is low OOP on river with minimal improvement potential (no draws). Folding preserves our deep stack (12k+) for better spots in a long 150-hand tournament, avoiding a tough river decision where we're likely behind. Consistent with pot control strategy from checking turn.

I am holding Pocket Kings (Kc Ks), which is the second-best starting hand in poker. I am in the UTG position with a stack of 3,135 chips (approx. 31 big blinds). I want to open for a standard raise to build the pot and extract value while also trying to narrow the field. A raise to 300 has been my standard opening size in this tournament so far, and it is appropriate here.

QTo offsuit is a marginal hand facing an UTG raise from Flash, who has a short stack of ~28BB but still represents strength from early position. Preflop, with SB and BB yet to act after me, calling risks multiway pots or a squeeze, and the hand doesn't play well postflop without position advantage outweighing the range disadvantage. No strong blockers or playability justifies a 3bet. Deep stack allows waiting for better spots to maximize EV over 150 hands. Standard fold to preserve chips.

2-3 offsuit is the worst starting hand in poker with virtually zero equity. Despite decent pot odds, this hand should be folded from any position. With Minni still to act from Big Blind and a short-stacked Flash in the pot, there's no scenario where 2-3o is a profitable play. Folding is the standard decision.

Call 300 (putting 200 more). A♣3♠ is marginal and dominated by a UTG opener's stronger ace combos, but the price to see a flop is good (about 30–31% pot odds). I don’t want to 3‑bet here — Flash is relatively short (~28 BB) and a 3‑bet could get me shoved on, forcing difficult dominated calls. Folding would be too nitty given the cheap price. Plan postflop: proceed cautiously out of position — fold to large aggression unless I improve to at least top pair or a strong draw; realize equity on flops and control pot size when I miss.

Flop: 5♠ A♠ 3♥ — I hold A♣3♠: two pair (Aces and Threes). This is a very strong made hand on this dry board, but it is vulnerable to a few specific hands that beat me (A5, pocket 5s, pocket 33). Flash opened UTG to 300 and has a ~28BB stack (≈2835), so his raising range contains many Ax and pocket pairs; with his short-ish stack he can also shove pot-sized stacks. Pot is 650 and no bet yet. Strategy: extract value and deny equity — lead out for a value bet that (a) charges worse Aces and medium pocket pairs and (b) keeps the size small enough to avoid gratuitously bloating the pot or committing myself to a huge call on a shove. I will bet 400 (≈0.6 pot). If Flash shoves behind I will call — two pair is ahead of most of his shove range and calling is correct vs a wide short-stack jam; if he just calls I keep control and re-evaluate on the turn. Betting also prevents giving a free card to hands that can improve to beat me.

The flop brought the Ace of Spades, which is the most dangerous card for my Pocket Kings. Minni's decision to lead out (donk bet) for 400 into the pre-flop aggressor strongly suggests she has an Ace in her range, or perhaps a two-pair or straight draw that currently has me beaten. Given the high probability that I am behind and the fact that I am drawing to only two outs if she has an Ace, I will fold to preserve my stack for a better spot.

Preflop UTG 4-handed with Qc4d offsuit. This is a trash hand with poor high card value, no suitedness, and weak playability postflop. Even in a shortened table, standard range doesn't include this from early position. Deep effective stacks, better to preserve chips for stronger hands. Clear fold to wait for premium spots and maximize long-term EV.

5-10 offsuit is a weak hand with poor playability. Despite good button position, calling 100 into two invested opponents is not profitable. The hand lacks high cards, pairs, or connectivity. Folding marginal hands preflop preserves stack for stronger opportunities. This is standard exploitative play against most opponent ranges.

Only the big blind remains, so I'm heads-up. 6c7d is playable heads-up and worth an open to steal/isolate. I’ll make a small standard open to 200 total (min-raise) — it’s a low-risk way to deny a cheap flop, isolate Flash, and set up C-bets on favorable boards. If he 3-bets big or shoves I’ll fold this weak offsuit; if called I’ll c-bet appropriate textures and reevaluate on the turn.

I am in the Big Blind with J7o. Minni has raised from the Small Blind to 200, making it 100 more for me to call. The pot odds are 3:1 (100 to win 300). In a blind-vs-blind situation, J7o is strong enough to defend, especially since I will have position on Minni for the rest of the hand. Minni has been very active and aggressive, so her raising range is likely wide. I will call to see a flop.

I was the preflop raiser heads-up with 6c7d and completely missed the Qh Th 4s flop. Versus a wide defending range from the big blind, a continuation bet is the standard play to fold out unpaired overcards and small pairs and to seize the initiative. The board is relatively dry (only two hearts, no obvious straight completes), and Flash is short-stacked (~2.6k), so a small-to-medium c-bet puts pressure without committing stacks. I’ll make a ~half-pot c-bet to take it down now; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (pot-control on blanks, fold to a large raise/check-raise since I have no real showdown value).

I am in the Big Blind with Jack-Seven offsuit. The flop of Queen-Ten-Four does not connect with my hand in any significant way. I have no pair and no real draw. Minni, who raised from the Small Blind, has continued with a bet of 240 into a 400 pot. Given that the board is quite coordinated and favors a Small Blind opening range, and I have zero showdown value or equity, there is no reason to continue.

K5o (King-Five offsuit) from UTG is a weak, easily dominated hand that should be folded from early position. Standard poker strategy dictates playing only premium and strong hands like high pairs (TT+), AK, AQ, and occasional AJ+ from UTG. With a healthy stack (15,640), there's no urgency to play marginal holdings. The blinds will come around regularly, so passing on weak hands preserves chips for premium spots where I have a mathematical edge. This is a straightforward fold.

I’m on the Button with K♣? sorry K♥8♠ (K8 offsuit) and deep stacks (~89BB). This is a reasonable spot to steal the blinds and take initiative: calling is too passive (no fold equity) and a tiny raise looks weak. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x the big blind) to apply pressure and either take the pot down or play post‑flop in position. If I face a shove from the short SB (~25BB) I’ll fold K8o; if called I’ll c‑bet favorable flops and use position to control the pot.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Seven offsuit. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. This is a weak starting hand to play out of position, and my stack is relatively short compared to the rest of the table. Calling here would put me in a difficult spot post-flop against a wide button range. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after UTG fold and SB fold. My hand is 9d7s offsuit, a weak speculative hand with poor playability, low equity against a wide button opening range (which includes many better broadways, pairs, and suited connectors). Pot odds are 200 to call into 450 (~2.25:1, requiring ~31% equity), but 97o realizes poorly post-flop out of position, gets dominated often, and doesn't block strong hands. Deep stacks (mine 127x BB, Minni 86x), but no reason to defend trash here. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this long tournament.

Fold. From UTG in a 4‑hand game 7♣5♠ offsuit is an unplayable, easily dominated holding. Calling 100 to see a flop leaves me out of position against the blinds and the button and often in multiway pots where my hand has very little equity. Raising is unreasonable with this hand. With deep stacks at the table and opponents who open-raise fairly frequently, preserving chips and waiting for a much better spot is the correct long‑term play.

I am currently the shortest stack at the table with approximately 26 big blinds. While 98o is a potential opening hand from the button in a 4-handed game to steal the blinds, my current position and the aggression of the players in the blinds (Elon and Claude) make this a risky spot. Elon and Claude have both shown a willingness to defend or play aggressively post-flop. Given that 98o is at the bottom of an opening range and I want to preserve my stack for a better opportunity to double up or move all-in, I will choose to fold and wait for a stronger starting hand.

KQs is a strong suited broadway hand with excellent equity and playability heads-up against the big blind's wide range. With both UTG and Button folded, this is a standard spot to raise from the small blind to steal the dead money in the pot or build the pot with position post-flop if called. Raising to 3x the big blind (300) is a balanced size that pressures the BB while keeping pot manageable. Folding or limping would be suboptimal.

I'm in the big blind with 6d4d, one of the weakest possible hands in poker. Elon raised from the small blind to 300. While I'm getting 2.5:1 odds to call 200 more into a 400 pot, 64o has terrible equity against any reasonable raising range from the small blind. This hand struggles even against a loose range and has no connectivity or broadway cards. With my healthy stack of 15,540, there's no reason to get involved in a marginal situation with such a poor holding. Folding preserves my chips for hands where I actually have an advantage.

I am holding Ace-Eight offsuit (Ah 8s) in the UTG position (which is effectively the Cutoff in a 4-handed game). With a stack of 2585 (approximately 26 big blinds), this is a standard opening hand. My goal is to pick up the blinds or see a flop with a range advantage. A standard raise to 300 has been my consistent sizing throughout the tournament and remains appropriate here.

68o is a trash hand with poor playability and equity against an UTG raise from Flash, even with his shorter stack of ~23BB. My position on the button is advantageous postflop, but calling 300 into a 450 pot commits resources to a speculative hand unlikely to realize equity multiway against strong ranges. With deep effective stacks (my 127xBB), discipline is key early in the tournament (hand 37/150) to maximize stack growth by playing premium spots. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities.

I'm holding 5s3c (one of the weakest hands in poker) in the small blind facing Flash's raise to 300 from UTG. While Flash is short-stacked (2285 remaining), several factors make folding the correct play: (1) This is an extremely weak hand with minimal equity against any reasonable raising range; (2) I'm out of position with no post-flop flexibility; (3) To call 250 more, I'm getting roughly 1.8:1 pot odds (450 to call 250), which is insufficient for a hand this weak; (4) Minni in the big blind still needs to act, and could re-raise or complicate the situation; (5) This is a classic example of a hand that loses value when played. I should preserve my 15,490 chip stack for better opportunities rather than get involved with such a marginal holding out of position.

I have AA in the big blind facing a UTG open to 300. This is the best possible starting hand — I want to isolate the raiser and extract value while putting pressure on a relatively short stack (Flash ~2.3k). A small call lets them see a cheap flop and invites dominated hands to stay; a tiny 3-bet is too weak. I will 3-bet to 1500 total. That size is large enough to charge broadways and mid pairs, and is likely to induce a shove or a thin call from worse hands; if he shoves I will call (AA is a clear favorite). If he folds I take down the 450 pot. Postflop I’ll continue aggressively on safe textures; overall this balances value extraction and fold equity against a single opener.

I opened to 300 with Ah 8s, a standard play from the Cutoff/UTG. Minni responded with a very large 3-bet to 1500 (5x my open). At 23 BB deep, this sizing is extremely polar or indicative of a premium hand. A8o does not play well against this range, and calling would leave me with less than 800 chips (8 BB) in a pot of over 3000. Shoving for my remaining 1985 chips is an overplay given the strength shown. Folding allows me to preserve a 20 BB stack and wait for a better spot to commit my chips.

4-handed NLHE, UTG with Ad7d (suited Ace, good playability, in raising range UTG 4-handed). First to act preflop, blinds posted (pot 150). Standard open-raise sizing around 3x BB to 300 builds pot, isolates short-stack Flash (2185 chips) who might shove weak hands, and applies pressure on Minni/Claude. Folding too tight, limping invites multiway pots OOP bad. Raising maximizes EV long-term.

K5o is a marginal hand that doesn't warrant playing against an early position raise from Elon, despite my positional advantage on the button. Elon's UTG raise from early position indicates genuine strength (likely a pocket pair, broadway cards, or AK/AQ range). While position is valuable, K5 is too weak to justify calling and playing out of position for the rest of the hand. The pot (450) doesn't offer sufficient incentive to defend a weak hand. There are still two opponents to act (Minni in SB and Flash as short stack BB), which complicates the situation further. Folding preserves my stack for better opportunities where my hand strength better matches my investment.

Fold. Qc3c in the small blind facing an UTG open to 300 is too weak to defend. Calling 250 would leave me out of position against an UTG range that dominates Q3s and likely produce a multiway pot with the short-stacked BB behind who can shove or fold — not a profitable spot. A light reraise to 500 is thin and invites a shove or a call from better hands. Pot odds (250 into 450 → ~36% equity required) and reverse implied odds don’t justify calling. Preserve my 9,400 stack for better spots — fold.

I am in the big blind with 8d 7h. Elon raised to 300 from UTG. My current stack is approximately 22 big blinds. While the pot odds are roughly 2.25:1, 87-offsuit is a weak hand to play out of position against an UTG opener. Given my stack size, it is better to fold and wait for a stronger opening or a better spot to defend.

UTG position requires strong hands. Q3o is too weak to open from the worst position, especially against aggressive opponents like Elon (big blind) and Minni (button). Folding preserves my stack for premium hands in better spots. This maintains my tight, selective image which has been profitable in earlier hands.

I have Q7o on the button with only the blinds left to act and the pot only 150 (to call 100). This is a prime spot to open-steal: the small blind is relatively short (Flash 2135) and the big (Elon) will defend but I keep positional advantage if called. Standard sizing at this table has been ~3x the BB, so I’ll raise to 300 to maximize fold equity while risking a small portion of my 9400 stack. If called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and give up to heavy 3-bet aggression (folding to large 3-bets) since Q7o is marginal.

I am in the Small Blind with J2o, which is a very weak hand. Minni has raised from the button to 300. Given my relatively short stack of 21bb and the fact that I would be playing out of position with a bottom-tier hand, there is no reason to defend. Folding is the standard and most prudent play here to preserve my remaining chips for a better opportunity.

AKo is one of the best hands in poker, especially against a button raise which is typically a wide range including many Ax hands worse than mine, broadway cards, suited connectors, etc. Minni has been aggressive with frequent raises from late position throughout the history, often isolating or stealing blinds. Facing a standard 3x BB raise after folds, this is a clear 3-bet spot to isolate, build the pot with value, and deny equity to her weaker holdings. Sizing to 1000 (about 3.3x her raise) is standard pot-building size given deep effective stacks (mine 12k, hers 9k), leaving room for further action postflop. Calling is too passive as it invites multiway pots or her check-raising flops; folding is never correct. No cooler shove needed this deep.

Elon 3-bet me to 1000 after my button open to 300. The pot is 1350 and I must call 700, so I need ~34% equity to break even. Q7o is heavily dominated by a typical 3-bet range and doesn’t have that equity often enough. A 4-bet bluff (min 1700) is thin — I don’t have the right blockers and Elon is deep enough to call or shove, putting me in a worse spot. Calling to see a flop keeps position, but I’ll face very difficult postflop decisions with a marginal hand. Better to preserve my 9100 stack and wait for a clearer spot to exploit. Fold.

UTG with pocket Aces (Ah Ad) in a 4-handed game: this is the best hand and I want to build the pot and isolate weaker ranges while keeping SPR reasonable. Standard, non-nitty open sizing here is 3x the big blind — raise to 300 — which prices out many speculative hands and forces short-stack Button (Flash, ~21BB) into tough decisions (likely shove or fold). I'm comfortable calling a shove from Flash or a reasonable 3-bet from the deep stacks (Elon/Claude) and will re-evaluate action-specific responses (4-bet or call) if they 3-bet large. Raising to 300 now maximizes value and protects my hand preflop.

I am on the button with Ac Jc, which is a premium hand in a 4-handed game. Minni raised to 300 from UTG. With a stack of 2135 (approx 21 BB), I want to be aggressive here to either take down the pot pre-flop or isolate Minni in position. A 3-bet to 900 is a standard sizing that exerts significant pressure while building a pot for a strong hand. If I am 4-bet shoved on, I will have to evaluate based on the odds, but AJs is certainly strong enough to 3-bet for value here.

Preflop in the small blind with J6s suited, a speculative hand. Facing an UTG raise from Minni (tight-aggressive tendencies from history) and a button 3-bet from Flash (who can be wide on button but still strong range). Out of position postflop, pot odds to call 850 into 1350 (~38% equity needed, but vs 3-bet range OOP my equity is low ~25-30%). Folding preserves stack for better spots in multi-way pots or vs weaker ranges. No fold equity squeeze viable here with 4 players and BB to act. Prioritize stack maximization over marginal calls.

This is a straightforward fold. I hold 3h-9c, one of the weakest hands in poker, facing a 3x re-raise out of position. Minni raised to 300 from UTG, and Flash aggressively re-raised to 900, which indicates a strong range (likely medium+ pairs or premium big cards like AK). My 3-9 offsuit has minimal equity against this aggression and I'm out of position post-flop. With a healthy stack (15390) and 110 hands remaining, there's no value in calling 800 chips with such a weak hand facing positional and distribution disadvantages. This is a standard fold that preserves chip equity for better opportunities.

I have pocket Aces (AH AD) and opened to 300. Flash (Button) 3-bet to 900 but has a short stack (1235) — only ~335 left behind. With everyone else folded, this is a high EV spot to commit chips: I want to isolate the short stack and get called by worse hands (paired hands, AK, AQ, etc.). Calling 600 more keeps SPR large and can concede value; a small 4-bet to the min (1500) just forces him all‑in anyway because he’s short. I’ll 4‑bet shove to maximize value and freeze action — he’ll be all‑in for ~1235 or fold, and AA is a big favorite in that matchup. Action: 4‑bet all‑in to extract maximum value from Flash’s 3‑bet range.

Minni has 4-bet shoved over my 3-bet. I have Ac Jc and have already committed 900 chips, leaving me with only 1235 chips behind. The pot is currently 9850 and it costs me 1235 to call. This gives me pot odds of approximately 8:1, meaning I only need about 11% equity to make this a mathematically correct call. Even against her strongest possible range (like AA or KK), I have enough equity to justify the call. I am pot-committed and cannot fold getting these odds with a premium hand like AJs.

Preflop on the button with Ks5c. Pot 150, to call 100 (limp), min-raise to 200. With 3 players and action folded to me (SB likely folded, BB checked), this is a prime spot to open wide from the button against the BB. K5o is a profitable open: king-high blocker, decent high-card strength, good playability postflop in position. Deep stacks (~130BB) favor raising for initiative, fold equity, and building pot OOP opponent's likely wide defending range. Standard sizing 3x BB to 300 balances value and bluffs effectively based on observed aggression levels.

K6 offsuit is a weak hand that plays poorly out of position in the small blind. While I'm only facing 250 more to call into a 450 pot (roughly 1.8:1 odds), the equity of K6o against Elon's button-raise range is insufficient. Additionally, Minni in the big blind still has to act, and there's potential for a re-raise that would put me in a worse spot. Elon has shown reasonable aggression from the button, but K6o is too marginal to continue with. This is a standard fold from the small blind against a raise with a weak unpaired hand. Preserving chips for better spots is the priority in a 150-hand tournament.

Elon opened to 300 from the button — a standard 3x open. Q♢9♣ is too weak and ill-suited for a light 3-bet out of position (I lack the value or clear fold equity to pressure him profitably). Calling 200 more (300 total committed) is the best option: it defends my blind against a likely wide BTN range while keeping the pot manageable out of position. Postflop I’ll fold to heavy aggression when completely missed, but proceed with top pair, good second pair, or strong draws. Preserve stack depth for better spots rather than bloating the pot preflop with a marginal hand.

I called preflop with Q♦9♣ and the flop A♥ J♣ 3♥ completely misses my hand — no pair and only a remote backdoor club draw. Elon (BTN) checked, which can be either weakness or pot control with a real Ace/J; out of position I don’t want to barrel a marginal hand and inflate a pot I’m unlikely to win. Pot is 650 and stacks are deep, so I’ll exercise pot control: check and fold to heavy aggression unless I pick up real equity on the turn (pair, good draw or a free card that improves my relative strength).

Flop board A♥ 3♥ J♣ is somewhat coordinated with a flush draw possible and broadway potential, but my button opening range has decent A,J,x blockers (Ks5c blocks some Kx/Ax). Minni's BB calling range is wide and capped (few AA/KK sets, more Ax, suited broadways, suited connectors). Her check invites a c-bet, and with position, I can bet for value/thin value with king-high (beats her bluffs/missed draws) and fold equity against weaker holdings. Deep stacks allow controlled pot-building. Sizing 40-50% pot (650 pot) at 400 extracts value from worse while denying equity to her draws/floaters. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws and misses chances to win vs weak range. Long-term EV positive to c-bet wide IP here.

I checked the flop with Q♦9♣ and missed the A♥ J♣ 3♥ board. Elon opened from the button (likely a wide BTN range) and now fires a relatively small 400 into the 1050 pot. Calling 400 requires only ~28% equity and is a cheap way to see a turn with a deep stack — I have some backdoor/back-runner outs (and can still pair a Q or 9), but no reason to inflate the pot or bluff-raise out of position. My plan: call to realize equity and evaluate the turn — continue only if I pair or pick up a meaningful draw, otherwise fold to significant turn aggression. A fold now is too nitty given the price; a raise would be spewy with no value. Call 400.

Board: 3h Jc Ah 6c. My hand Qd9c has no pair and no realistic draw — only 6 thin outs to pair on the river, which will frequently still be second-best against Ax/Jx or better. I am out of position vs a BTN who c-bet the flop, so pot control is the priority. Betting or raising here would be a thin, low-expected-value bluff with little fold equity and poor blockers. I'll check to keep the pot small and fold to meaningful river aggression; if Elon checks back I can take a cheap showdown and reevaluate on the river.

Turn board brings the 6♣, adding a second club flush draw to an already draw-heavy board (A♥3♥J♣6♣). My K♠5♣ is king-high with no pair, no draw, and gutshot? No straight draw (K-high misses). Preflop I raised wide BTN, c-bet flop for initiative/value vs Minni's wide BB defending range (Ax weak, Jx, suited gappers/connectors, pairs below J). She check-called flop, capping her range somewhat (few sets/traps, more ATs+, KQs, 87s-type floaters/draws). Her turn check suggests weakness: either weak Ax showdown, missed draws, or trapping slowplayed strength (AJ/AQ/AK less likely given checks). Pot 1450, deep stacks (~120BB eff). Betting risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand; she can fold worse Ax/bluffs, call/raise better. King-high has showdown value vs her polarized calling range and blocks some of her top ends. Checking back: pot control, realizes equity cheaply, denies her free river cards on draws (though some complete), keeps initiative for river bluff/value if checkback. Superior to betting thin (negative EV if she folds >~40% worse) or overbetting. Long-term: balances my wide range, exploits her passivity from history (folds to aggression postflop often). Check preserves stack for better spots over 150 hands.

Check. I have Q♦9♣ on 3♥ J♣ A♥ 6♣ 4♥ — no pair, no real draw, just Q-high. Elon c-bet the flop then checked turn (and checked/freed up the river), which is consistent with pot-control with Ax/Jx or a missed float; my hand has very little showdown value and poor blockers for a reliable bluff. The pot is only 1,450 and stacks are deep, so betting as a thin bluff is low EV and exposes me to a call from better one-pair hands or a river raise. I’ll take the free showdown by checking and fold if Elon raises.

River 4h completes obvious heart flush draws (A♥3♥4♥) and adds straight possibilities (e.g., 2-5), but my K♠5♣ remains king-high with no improvement, blocking AK/AQ/KQ but vulnerable to Ax, Jx, flushes, and any two-pair+. Minni's line—BB defend to BTN raise (capped, wide: weak Ax, Jx, suited connectors/hearts)—check-call flop c-bet (thin value vs her range), check turn (weakness or trap), check river (passivity confirmed from history of check-downs/folds to aggression postflop). Pot 1450, deep stacks (~100BB+ eff.). Betting thin (e.g., 700-1000) risks folding out weaker Ax/Jx/bluffs (~40% needed for EV) while she calls/raises better (flush/AJ); bluffing pure air unbalanced after turn check. Checking maximizes EV: pot control OOP effectively IP here, realizes cheap showdown value vs her capped/passive range (beats missed draws/Jx <K), induces bluffs if she had semi-bluff intentions earlier, denies free realization to any lingering equity. Consistent with turn check for balance/exploitation of her history (e.g., Hands 1,3,6,12: frequent check-checks). Preserves stack for superior spots in 150-hand tournament.

I'm on the button with A♦9♥, a solid above-average hand. The pot is 150 with only 100 to call from the small blind and big blind. This is an excellent opportunity to raise for several reasons: (1) I have positional advantage as the button, (2) A9 is a strong enough hand to play aggressively, (3) Raising to 300 is a standard 3x raise that applies pressure and can win the blinds immediately, (4) If called, I'll have excellent position post-flop with a good holding. The raise size of 300 (3BB) is standard and balanced - not too small to be exploitable and not too large to risk unnecessary chips. Given the stack sizes and opponent tendencies (both Elon and Minni are aggressive), this aggressive play from the button is mathematically sound.

I have 8♣3♥ in the small blind facing a button open to 300 (pot 450). This is one of the worst hands preflop: unsuited, unconnected, and very poor postflop playability. Calling 250 OOP to play a multiway pot against the opener and the big blind would be dominated and requires ~36% equity to break even — 8-3 offsuit has far less vs a button opening range. 3-betting would be reckless with this holding. Preserve my stack and fold to wait for a much better spot.

3-handed, BB facing BTN raise after SB fold. JTo offsuit is a standard defensive calling hand with excellent pot odds (200 to call into 450 pot, ~30% implied). BTN range is wide (~40-50% of hands), giving JTo solid equity (~45-50%) heads-up and good playability for straights, two-pair, etc. Deep stacks allow realizing equity post-flop. OOP but manageable by playing selectively. Folding too tight, raising too strong for this hand.

Heads-up on the flop OOP as BB vs BTN raiser. Board is 5c-5h-9c, paired fives with two clubs, creating a dry but drawy board (flush draw possible). JcTs blocks some broadway and has a backdoor club flush draw plus backdoor straight potential (e.g., Qx river), but no made hand, pair, or strong draw currently. Equity realization is poor OOP with a marginal hand that rarely improves to top pair or better and gets outdrawn often. BTN range is wide (c-bet 50-70% here for value/protection), but my hand has too little showdown value or fold equity to bet OOP. Checking is standard: induces bluffs, controls pot, and allows folding to aggression without spew. Deep stacks don't change this; pot odds postflop won't be great against wide range. Folding preflop would be too tight given position and odds, but now check-fold to c-bet is GTO-ish unless tiny bet.

Continuation bet on the flop with a pair of 9s and an Ace kicker. I was the pre-flop aggressor and have position. Elon checked, indicating likely weakness or uncertainty. A bet of 450 (roughly 70% of the 650 pot) is standard sizing that maintains momentum and either wins the pot or gives me information about Elon's hand strength. The pair of 9s with Ace kicker is a reasonable holding, and continuing aggression is more profitable than checking and giving Elon a free turn card.

Facing a 450 bet into 1100 pot (~40% pot) OOP as BB vs BTN with JcTs on 5c-5h-9c flop. Hand has no pair, no strong draw (only backdoor broadway and weak backdoor club via Jc), low showdown value. BTN range wide from preflop raise (top ~30-40%), c-bets frequently here for value (pairs 5-9+, overpairs), protection, or bluffs. Equity ~35-45% vs range but poor postflop realization OOP: rarely hits top pair+, loses to flush/straight outs, thin value gets stacked. Pot odds ~22% but implied odds limited by paired board reducing villain's floats and my reverse implied when behind. Raising air unprofitable (no fold equity vs calling range). Check-fold standard GTO play to avoid spew deepstacked; preserves stack for better spots over 150 hands.

Button with 2♦4♦ and 106 BB effective. This is a marginal but playable suited hand in position against only the blinds — I want initiative and fold equity rather than passively calling into two deep stacks. Standard 3x open (300) is the right size here: it pressures the blinds, avoids giving them correct pot odds, and lets me play postflop in position if called. If I face a sizable 3‑bet I’ll fold this hand; if called I’ll c-bet dry boards and play cautiously on coordinated/paired runouts.

Minni is an aggressive raiser from the button, often opening wide ranges based on her history of frequent preflop raises and calls. KTo offsuit plays well as a 3-bet bluff/semi-bluff here from the small blind: it has solid equity against her broad opening range (top pair potential, straight draws, overcard blocker with the king), blocks strong king hands like KK/AK, and we can win the 450 pot immediately ~50%+ of the time if she folds. Standard sizing is 3x her raise (900 total). If called, we have position on Claude if he calls and decent playability OOP vs Minni. Folding OOP marginal hands preserves stack for better spots; calling is poor due to out of position postflop.

With pocket aces, the best starting hand in poker, I'm re-raising to build value. The action shows aggression from both opponents - Minni opened to 300, Elon 3-bet to 900. A re-raise to 2500 (approximately 2.75x Elon's bet) is a standard aggressive play that extracts value from both players' likely holdings while keeping them engaged. This maintains my advantage and builds the pot significantly preflop with the strongest hand possible.

I opened to 300 with 2♦4♦ but now face Elon’s 3‑bet to 900 and Claude’s 4‑bet to 2500. The pot is 3700 and it costs me 2200 to call (pot would be ~5900), so I’d need about 37% equity to make a call + play reasonable. 2♦4♦ has far less equity versus a polarized/value 4‑bet range and is frequently dominated. 5‑betting or shoving is committing a large portion of my 10335 stack with virtually no fold equity or equity advantage. Even in position this is a clear fold — preserve the stack and wait for a better spot.

Facing a 4-bet to 2500 from Claude in the BB after my 3-bet bluff with KTo offsuit vs Minni's button open. Minni has folded, leaving us heads-up OOP postflop. Claude's 4-bet range is likely tight (JJ+/AK/AQ), against which KTo has poor equity (~25-30% vs top range) and is often dominated by AK/KQ. Pot odds require ~23% equity to call 1600 into 5300, but playability is bad OOP with offsuit blockers-only hand, no strong draws or made value. 5-bet jamming or raising commits too much stack (~25% effective) with marginal bluff equity given Claude's aggression history. Folding preserves our deep stack (12775) for better spots; we represented strength with the 3-bet and got value from Minni's fold.

3-handed on the button first to act pre-flop with T8o. This hand has decent playability with straight potential and a ten for overcard value. In short-handed play, opening range from BTN is very wide. Standard raise size of 3x the BB to 300 to build the pot or steal blinds. Position allows controlling post-flop.

I'm in the small blind facing Elon's 300 raise with K4o (Four of Clubs, King of Spades). This is a weak hand that doesn't warrant continuing in this spot. Key factors: (1) K4 offsuit is a marginal holding with limited win equity, (2) I'm out of position in the SB, which is unfavorable post-flop, (3) I have a healthy stack (16,840) with 106 hands remaining - no urgency to take marginal spots, (4) Elon's button raise could represent a wide range, and even if I flop a king, I'm vulnerable to better kickers. With a solid stack and many hands to play, this is a good spot to fold and conserve chips for stronger holdings.

I’m on the big blind with pocket 7s (7♠7♥) facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and it costs me 200 more to call (300 total contribution). With ~102BB effective stacks, pocket 7s are best used as a set-mining hand versus a single raiser from the button — calling preserves stack depth and gives good pot/implied odds to hit a set and get paid. A small 3-bet here would have thin fold equity and would commit chips out of position unnecessarily. Plan: call to see the flop; proceed aggressively if I hit a set or a very favorable board, otherwise proceed cautiously OOP.

Check. I have pocket 7s on a Q♠ 6♦ 2♠ flop — an underpair to the queen with two spades on board. I called preflop to set-mine and missed; stacks are deep (~100BB), so I don’t want to barrel out of position with a marginal hand that has little fold equity. Checking controls the pot, allows me to realize equity and see a turn cheaply (possible set or favorable runouts), and keeps my range wide for future plays. If Elon bets small I’ll often call and re-evaluate on the turn; I’ll fold to large, polarized aggression unless I improve. For now: check.

3-handed on button vs BB defender who checked flop. My preflop raising range is wide and includes many Qx, overcards, and draws. Board is Qs6d2s: somewhat coordinated with flush draw but dry low cards. T8o has ten overcard, backdoor spade draw (8s), and good playability. No showdown value, but position allows profitable c-bet bluff here. Minni has defended wide pre and checks often post-flop based on history, folding frequently to aggression (e.g., Hands 5,25,33,34). C-bet ~60% pot (400) pressures her range while keeping pot manageable if called. Fold equity high, or if check-raise, I can fold equity well.

Call 400. I called preflop to set-mine and this flop (Qs 6d 2s) is exactly the sort of spot where I want to keep the pot controllable and realize equity OOP. Elon’s 400 is a small c-bet into ~1050, consistent with a wide continuation range (Qx, overcards, small pairs, spade draws). With an underpair, raising OOP has thin fold equity and needlessly bloats the pot out of position (min-raise here would be to 800). Calling preserves stack depth and keeps worse hands and bluffs in his range while giving me implied odds to hit a set. Pot odds for this call are ~27–28%; pure chances to make a set or backdoor flush by the river (~8–14% combined) don’t fully justify a call on raw equity alone, but the implied payoff if I hit a set makes a call + correct. Plan: call the flop, reassess on the turn — if I hit a 7 or improve strongly I’ll play aggressively; if the turn bricks and Elon raises large I’ll fold to avoid paying off for a marginal underpair.

Check. I have 7♠7♥ on 6♦-2♠-Q♠-6♣: a single pair that currently loses to Qx and any 6 (which makes trips). Elon checked the turn, which looks like pot control with top pair or a missed/draw-heavy holding rather than a polarized monster. Betting OOP here has very thin value — few worse hands will call and I can get multi-way or committed against hands that already beat me (6x, Qx). With ~96BB effective and a 1,450 pot, I want to keep the pot manageable and realize my equity rather than bloating the pot out of position. Plan is to check to the river, re-evaluate if I improve (bet/raise for value with a 7) or if the river bricks decide on a check-call or fold depending on sizing and opponent aggression.

Turn board pairs the 6s, bricking most flop draws like spades, making my overcard T8s even better as a bluff. Preflop raiser range includes QQ, 66+, Q6s, some Ax overcards. Minni's check-call flop range is capped somewhat (Qx, 66, slowplayed sets), but history shows she folds often to continued aggression (e.g., Hands 5,13,25,33,34). No showdown value, position strong, pot 1450 with deep stacks. Bet ~60% pot (900) maintains pressure, high fold equity against her likely marginal hands, builds pot if she folds weaker Qx/pairs, or if called/raised, easy fold. Checking risks giving free card or looks weak after flop c-bet.

Fold. I have 7♠7♥ on a board 6♦ 2♠ Q♠ 6♣. Elon barreled the flop and now bets 900 into a 2350 pot — it costs me 900 to call, giving ~27.7% pot odds. I only have two clean outs (two remaining 7s) to make a set (~4.3% on the river), so my raw equity is far below the price. Elon opened preflop and continued on flop+turn, so his range is heavily weighted to Qx, hands containing a 6 (now trips), and stronger pairs — holdings that dominate my 7s. Raising out of position here has very thin fold equity and would unnecessarily bloat the pot with a marginal hand. My prior plan was to fold to large turn aggression on a blank — this is that spot. Preserve the stack and fold.

With 6h4h on the button, I'm facing a 100 chip call to a 150 chip pot with favorable odds and position advantage. However, this is a weak hand - an unsuited connector with no high cards and limited potential. While button position is valuable against blind opponents, 6h4h is below the threshold of hands I should be getting involved with at this stage of the tournament. My stack is healthy at 16,840 chips, and there's no need to take marginal situations with weak holdings. I'll fold and wait for stronger opportunities where I can leverage my positional advantage more effectively.

K♣8♠ is a playable heads-up hand and I want to take the initiative rather than just limp. Pot is 150 and calling 50 is passive; a standard open to 3×BB (300 total) gives me fold equity, builds the pot with a wide value/bluff range, and fits the table tendency to open to ~300. If Elon folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I have position postflop and can play a wide C-bet/float strategy; if he 3-bets large I can fold or re-evaluate based on sizing and reads. Raise to 300 to steal the blind or play the flop in position.

AK diamond suited is a premium hand, perfect for 3-betting preflop for value and isolation against a small blind raise, which is often wide-ranging given the history of frequent opens from Minni. Stacks are deep enough (effective ~9300 from Minni), so a standard 3x raise to 900 total commits appropriately without overexposing. This builds the pot with our equity advantage and fold equity if she has a marginal hand.

I opened to 300 with K♣8♠ but Elon 3-bet to 900. Facing a 3-bet heads-up and out of position, K8o has poor equity against a typical 3-betting range (PP/AK/AQ/KQs/etc.) and suffers reverse implied odds if I hit a lone K. Calling 600 into a ~1200 pot (~33% pot odds) isn’t enough to overcome being dominated OOP, and a 4-bet bluff to the min (1500) is too thin and easily exploited. Preserve my 9k+ stack and wait for a better spot — fold here.

I’m on the button with 8♥5♣ (offsuit) in a three-handed pot. With both blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100) and deep stacks (~90+ BB), button open-raising wide is standard — 8-5o is marginal but playable from this position because I’ll have position postflop and can often steal the blinds. The table’s common open size is ~300, which gives good fold equity without overcommitting. Plan: raise to take the initiative; if I’m 3-bet big I’ll fold this marginal hand, if called I’ll C-bet favorable flops and use position to pick spots. Raise to 300 to maximize steal chances and keep pot manageable.

Q6o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive with preflop raises in previous hands. Defending the small blind wide OOP here risks difficult postflop spots with a hand that rarely makes strong hands. Pot odds are not compelling (calling 250 into 450 for ~1.8:1), and folding preserves the stack for better spots in this tournament phase.

Folding 4s 6h from the Big Blind against Minni's button raise. This is one of the weakest hands in poker with no real drawing potential or pair equity. Playing OOP with a trash hand against a button raiser is -EV long-term, even getting favorable pot odds. Preserving my 16,740-chip stack for better opportunities is the mathematically correct approach in a 150-hand tournament format.

3-handed on the button with 86s, a suited connector with good playability and potential for straights/flushes. Deep stacks, excellent position. Standard to open wide from button here to steal blinds or play postflop advantageously. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is a standard sizing to apply pressure and build the pot without overcommitting. Folding is too tight; limping is suboptimal as it invites multiway pots without initiative.

Folding Q-8o from the small blind against Elon's button raise. While Elon has shown a tendency to raise frequently from the button with a wide range, Q-8 offsuit is a weak, dominated hand that lacks the strength to profitably defend here. 

With 16,690 chips and no immediate stack pressure, I should preserve chips by folding marginal hands rather than playing them out of position against a player who seems aggressive. The 250 to call isn't worth the equity disadvantage in this spot. Standard fold.

Minni still needs to act in the big blind, so there's also risk of a re-raise, which would make my call look even worse.

I have pocket fives in the big blind facing a BTN open to 300 (I have 100 posted; calling costs me 200). Stacks are deep (~94bb effective) and this is a classic heads-up set-mining / defensive-call spot. Elon’s button open is likely wide, so my pocket pair has good implied odds to hit a set or be ahead at showdown. A small 3-bet (to 500) would be too weak to generate true fold equity and a larger 3-bet would commit more chips out of position unnecessarily. I’ll call to see a flop, then proceed cautiously OOP — look to extract value if I hit a set or continue vs weak flops, otherwise avoid large bloated pots.

I called preflop as a set-mining defense, but this flop (9♦ 7♥ 9♠) completely misses me — I have an underpair (55). I only have two outs to a set (2/47 ≈ 4.3% on the turn, ≈ 8.5% by the river), so my equity is low. With deep stacks (~92bb) and out of position versus the button, I want to control the pot and avoid barreling without a made hand. I’ll check to keep the pot small — willing to call a small c-bet to realize equity, but will fold to a large bet or raise and re-evaluate on the turn if checked to.

Heads-up on a paired 9d-7h-9s flop after my button raise to 300, which Minni defended from BB. I hold 86s, giving me a spade flush draw (10 outs) plus a gutshot straight draw to the 10 (4 outs, minimal overlap), roughly 26-30% equity against her range. The board is somewhat coordinated but paired, hitting some of her calling range (99, 97s, 87s, pocket pairs below 99, draws). Minni checked OOP, standard. With position, deep stacks (mine 13k, hers 9k, pot 650), this is a prime semi-bluff spot: strong draw, fold equity against her weak check-back range, and we win big when we hit. C-bet half-pot ~325-400 to deny equity, build pot, apply pressure. Larger sizing risks check-raise but maximizes fold equity here given history of her calling but folding to aggression post-flop (e.g., Hand 5, 17, 25). Betting 400 balances value (if she has worse pair/draw) and semi-bluff.

Preflop I called as a set‑mine, but this flop (9♦ 7♥ 9♠) completely misses me — I’m an underpair (55) with only two outs. Elon’s bet is 400 into a 1,050 pot; calling 400 would make the pot 1,450, so I’m being offered ~27.6% pot odds (400/1450). My chance to hit a set by the river is only ~8.5%, far below the required equity. There’s essentially no fold equity for a raise and charging into position with a naked small pair is a poor line. Calling here is a negative‑EV chase and opening myself to larger OOP losses on later streets, so I’ll fold and preserve my ~92bb stack for better spots.

Jack-Three offsuit is a very weak hand, even from the button position. While I have positional advantage and decent pot odds (2.5:1 to call 100 into 150), the hand quality is poor—it has limited postflop playability, weak kicker, and minimal equity against likely opponent ranges. Minni and Elon have been moderately aggressive in recent hands. Folding weak hands and maintaining a tight, selective approach has been working well. This hand doesn't warrant risking chips preflop. Folding is the disciplined play.

Heads-up from the small blind with TdJh (offsuit J-T) — a very playable hand. With only the big blind left and a deep ~91 BB stack, I want to seize the initiative, apply pressure, and narrow Elon's calling range. A standard 2x BB open to 200 (total) gives good fold equity, keeps pot controllable if called, and allows me to c-bet a range on many flops. If Elon 3-bets large I can fold or re-evaluate based on sizing and reads; but open-raising is the default profitable play here.

T5o is a trash hand with poor postflop playability, especially out of position against the SB who acts first postflop. Minni's raise to 200 (roughly 2x) suggests a wide range, but T5o has low equity, gets dominated often by better T/x or 5/x, and doesn't make strong hands frequently. Pot odds are decent (300 pot, 100 to call), but implied odds are bad due to reverse position and hand's weakness. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this deep-stack tournament phase. No squeeze value with button folded.

I’m on the Button with A♣J♥ and ~92 BB effective — a very playable, high-equity hand in position. Folding is too weak and limping gives the blinds cheap odds and invites multiway action where AJo performs worse. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300) to take the initiative, deny good odds to callers and isolate the blinds. If I face a standard-sized 3-bet I’ll evaluate based on size (call vs medium 3-bet to play postflop in position; fold to a large 4-bet unless the price is right). Overall raise to 300 to build the pot and use position.

A2 offsuit is a weak hand with poor postflop playability, dominated by many stronger aces and lacking suited or connected value. Minni's button raise suggests a wide range, but calling 250 into a 450 pot offers insufficient equity long-term. Position is decent heads-up, but better to preserve stack for stronger hands in this short-handed game. Folding is the disciplined play to maximize stack over 150 hands.

Q-8 offsuit is a weak holding that struggles significantly out of position. While the pot odds (2.25:1, needing ~30% equity) are marginally acceptable, I have only ~40% equity against Minni's button opening range. More importantly, I'll be out of position post-flop against an aggressive opponent, which is where this hand becomes problematic. I'll be forced to play passively in a raised pot without the hand strength or positional advantage to execute a profitable strategy. With 16,590 chips and still 101 hands remaining, this is a clear fold. I can preserve chips for stronger spots where I have better equity and position.

3-handed on the button pre-flop with blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100). K3o is a marginal hand but playable in position with a wide button stealing range (~50%+ hands). King provides a decent high card blocker, and position allows controlling the pot post-flop. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard for value/thin value/steal against blinds who may fold weak hands. Minni has been aggressive but also called raises; Claude similar. Stacks are deep, so fold equity high. Calling is too passive; folding too tight.

A4o is a weak hand to play out of position in small blind. While I have an ace, the four kicker leaves me vulnerable to a wide range of hands from Elon's button raise. Additionally, Minni still has to act from big blind and could 3-bet. With a healthy 16,540 chip stack, there's no need to gamble with a marginal holding when better spots will surely come. This is a standard fold.

Elon opened to 300 from the button; I'm in the BB with J♣7♠ (J7 offsuit). Pot is 450 and it costs me 200 to call (total commitment 300) — about 30–31% pot odds. Effective stacks are deep (~93bb). J7o is marginal but defendable heads-up against a wide button opening range. A minimum 3-bet to 500 is possible as a bluff but J7o lacks good blockers and postflop playability to make that attractive from out of position. I’ll call the 200 to see a flop and play straightforward: continue with a pair or strong draw, otherwise control the pot or fold to large aggression.

I called preflop to see a flop with J♣7♠ vs a wide button open. Flop T♥-6♦-3♥ misses my hand: I have one overcard (J) but no hearts or real draw. With ~93bb effective stacks and a reasonable chance the opener c-bets wide, I prefer pot control and to realize equity rather than turning this into a big bluff or bloated pot. Checking lets me see how Elon proceeds — I’ll call small-to-medium continuation bets to see a turn (looking to pair the J or pick up a favorable runout) but will fold to large, polarized pressure (pot-sized or bigger) that usually represents a made hand. For now: check.

Heads-up on the flop with bottom pair (threes) and King kicker on a coordinated board (3h6dTh) with two hearts and possible straight draws. As preflop raiser in position, a continuation bet exploits my perceived range strength and fold equity against Minni's likely wide BB defending range. Minni checked, suggesting weakness or trapping, but history shows frequent checking. Board hits my stealing range lightly. Betting ~60% pot (400) for value from worse pairs/draws, protection against draws, and denial of equity. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and forfeits initiative. Stacks deep (13k/9k effective), so sizing controls pot while maintaining fold equity. Favorable spot to build pot with showdown value + equity.

Fold. I’m out of position with J♣7♠ on T♥6♦3♥ — I checked and Elon bets 400 into the 650 pot (pot now 1050). Calling 400 requires ~38% equity. My hand has only 6 clean outs to pair (three J’s + three 7’s) — roughly 24% to hit by the river — plus negligible backdoor possibilities, well below the needed equity. There’s no meaningful draw or blocker value to justify a bluff-raise, and with deep stacks I don’t want to inflate the pot OOP with a barely-made hand. Fold and wait for a better spot.

2-6 offsuit is the worst starting hand in poker. While my position on the button is advantageous, it doesn't create enough value to justify playing this hand. My equity against any reasonable range from the small and big blind is too weak. Even though I only need to call 100 more chips, the long-term expectation is negative. In a 150-hand tournament focused on maximizing stack size, I need to be selective with my hands and preserve chips for premium situations. Folding is the mathematically correct play.

Call 100. I’ve already posted the SB (50); calling the extra 50 to make my total contribution 100 is a cheap way to see a flop heads-up. Ten-six offsuit is marginal and I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position with a thin raise (min-raise to 200 would commit more and invite defense/3-bets). Plan postflop: play fit-or-fold — continue only with a pair of tens, strong pair+kicker, or a real draw; fold to significant aggression on dry flops.

Preflop in the big blind with As7d facing a small blind limp from Minni. A7o is a strong hand in this spot with position postflop, good playability, and ace-high blocker. Minni's limp suggests a wide, weak range. Raising isolates, builds the pot with value/thin value, and takes initiative. Standard raise size around 3x the big blind to 300 total. Effective stacks are deep (142BB), so no need for larger. Folding or checking is too passive given hand strength and history of Minni calling limps with marginal hands.

Fold. T6o is a marginal, poorly connected hand and I’m out of position to Elon's 3x raise from the BB. Calling 200 into a 400 pot gives me ~33% break-even equity, but T6o is frequently dominated by a raiser’s range (pairs, Broadway, better tens) and has poor playability postflop. Raising would bloat the pot out of position with a weak hand and is unnecessary with a 9k stack. I planned to limp-call a cheap spot, but the raise to 300 changes the math — conserve chips and wait for a better opportunity.

9♦5♣ is a weak, unsuited, poorly connected hand. This is folded-to-button situation (pot 150, 100 to call); calling (limping) requires ~40% equity to break even and raising with this junk is likely to get called or 3-bet by players who defend/raise frequently here. I prefer to conserve 90BB stack for better high-expected-value spots (broadways, suited connectors, pairs) rather than play a marginal hand out of position postflop against competent defenders. Fold and wait for a stronger opportunity to steal or isolate.

Preflop in the small blind with Q8o after button folds. Unopened pot heads-up vs BB. Q8o is a profitable raising hand from SB due to fold equity against BB defense range and decent playability/postflop equity (blocks Qx, straight potential). OOP but deep effective stacks (~140BB) mitigate risk. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) is standard sizing to build pot/steal blinds while keeping fold equity high. BB folds weak hands often enough for +EV. If called, can play postflop accordingly based on board.

I'm in the Big Blind with 2h-6s, one of the worst hands in poker. Elon raised to 300 from the Small Blind and Minni folded. I'm facing a 200 chip call to stay in the pot with a hand that has virtually no real strength.

While I'm getting 2-to-1 pot odds (200 to call into 400), this hand is fundamentally too weak. Against Elon's raising range, 2-6o plays poorly post-flop and has around 20-25% equity at best. I'm out of position and will struggle to win this pot even if I hit the flop - at best I'd make a weak pair with bad kicker.

The math doesn't work here. I need significantly better equity to justify calling out of position with such a marginal holding. This is a straightforward fold.

Preflop on the button with 4h6s offsuit, a very weak hand with poor playability, low cards, and no suited or connected potential. Pot is 150, to call 100 gives minimal pot odds (about 1.5:1), but this hand has very low equity against any reasonable limping or raising range from SB or BB. Opponents have shown aggression in prior hands, but no need to speculate or defend wide here. Deep stacks, but folding trash preserves stack for better spots. Standard fold preflop.

I'm holding 5♣2♠, which is the worst starting hand in poker (deuce-five offsuit). While I'm in the small blind and only need to call 50 more chips (getting 3:1 pot odds with 150 in the pot), several factors make folding the right decision: (1) This hand has no realistic winning potential - it can only make the worst possible straights or flushes; (2) I'll be out of position on all post-flop streets since the big blind acts first; (3) Even with favorable immediate odds, playing weak hands from the small blind out of position is not profitable long-term; (4) With a healthy stack of 16,390, I should be selective about which marginal situations I enter. I'll preserve my chips for better spots and fold this trash hand.

With 2s Jc (jack-deuce offsuit), I hold one of the weakest possible hands in poker. While I'm on the button with positional advantage, J2o does not warrant a call even at the discounted price of 100 more chips. The hand plays poorly post-flop and has minimal equity against likely holdings from the blinds.

My stack is healthy at 16,390 chips with 96 hands remaining, so I can afford to be selective. Folding weak hands from the button maintains proper discipline and preserves chips for stronger opportunities. This is a clear fold situation regardless of position.

Call 100. K♠T♣ is a reasonable hand heads-up and the price to defend the small blind is excellent — I only need to put in 50 more into a 150 chip pot. I’m out of position postflop, so I don’t want to inflate the pot unnecessarily with a marginal hand by raising and inviting a 3-bet from Elon, who has shown he can be aggressive. Calling keeps my range wide, gives good pot odds to see a flop, and preserves stack depth to play postflop (either c-bet favorable boards or fold to heavy pressure).

In the big blind facing a small blind limp from Minni, who frequently limps with a wide range based on hand history, T3o is a speculative hand with straight potential but poor high-card strength. With deep stacks and position post-flop heads-up, checking to realize equity on the flop for free is optimal rather than raising a marginal hand that plays poorly post-flop or folding when seeing a cheap card maximizes EV over the long tournament.

Flop: 7d 6s 2h. I hold K♠ T♣ — two overcards, no pair and essentially no real draws. With only one spade between my hand and the board a flush by river is impossible and any straight would be runner-runner, so my current equity is low. I’m out of position and the pot is still small (200); betting here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and expose me to check-raises or tough decisions. I’ll check and keep the pot under control — see a free turn and reassess: call small bets with implied fold equity or fold to heavy pressure, and take the lead if I pair a King or Ten.

T3o flop hits nothing: no pair, no flush draw, and only a weak backdoor straight draw at best (needing runner-runner 4-5 or J-Q, unlikely). The 7d6s2h board is fairly dry but coordinated enough for opponent draws/pairs. Minni limped from SB (wide range including many weak hands) and checked flop, suggesting weakness. With position out of play and deep stacks, checking behind controls the pot, avoids bloating with air, and allows free cards or cheap realization of any backdoor equity. Betting as a bluff risks her calling light given history of calling stations, and folding equity isn't high enough vs her range. Check preserves stack for better spots in long tournament.

I have top pair (K♠T♣) with a medium kicker in a heads-up spot after both players checked the flop and turn. The check-check line from Elon indicates a lot of weakness (missed overcards and small pairs) in his range, so I want to take the initiative and extract value from worse Kx and small pairs while keeping the pot controllable. A half-pot / small value bet (100 into a 200 pot) accomplishes that — it charges any draws, folds out pure air, and keeps my options open on the river. If called I will re-evaluate based on the river card; if I face a large raise I’ll assume a stronger K or two pair and fold or proceed cautiously.

My hand T3o completely misses the board: no pair, no straight draw (possible gutshots like 4-5 but unlikely), no flush draw (backdoor heart at best with Th). Board is 7d6s2hKh, coordinated with possible straights (58, 45, 83), pairs, and Kh likely hit Minni's limping range. Minni limped SB (wide), checked flop (weak or trapping), now small bets 100 into 300 pot on turn – thin value with Kx, top pair, or sets, or bluff but her history shows calling station tendencies without folding much. Calling gets poor implied odds with air, as future river won't improve me enough against her range, and pot commits further. Raising as bluff too thin vs her stack and possible calls. Deep stack tournament, folding marginal air preserves chips for premium spots over 150 hands.

I’m on the button with A♠4♥ (As 4h) in a 3‑handed spot. Pot is 150 and the call is 100 — this is a good situation to apply pressure from the button. A‑4 offsuit is a weak ace but it fares well heads‑up and gains value with initiative and position, so I’ll open‑raise to a standard 3xBB sizing (300) to steal the blinds and isolate. If I get called I’ll continuation‑bet frequently on dry/A‑high flops and exercise pot control or check‑back when I have a marginal pair or face heavy resistance. If either opponent 3‑bets large I will usually fold (or only continue to a small 3‑bet with specific pot‑odds/position considerations).

4s6d is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity against a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive in previous hands. Facing a 3x raise from the button out of position in the small blind, calling commits 250 into a 450 pot with a hand that flops strong hands rarely and plays poorly post-flop. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in this deep-stack tournament phase, avoiding difficult OOP spots with marginal equity.

I'm holding Ac8h in the Big Blind facing Minni's button raise. This is a strong hand with an Ace, and I have significant positional awareness. Analysis: (1) Minni has been aggressive throughout the tournament, consistently raising from the button - this is a continuation of her pattern, (2) I have a substantial chip advantage (16,290 vs 8,885), (3) Ac8h is a premium hand that should be played for value against her wide button-raising range, (4) Elon has folded, making this a heads-up situation where I can apply pressure, (5) Minni's short stack (about 29 BB) makes her vulnerable to well-timed aggression. A raise to 700 applies meaningful pressure while maintaining balance - I'm not over-committing, but I'm clearly signaling strength and taking the initiative. This leverages my stack advantage and the strength of my holding.

Claude has 3‑bet to 700 after I opened to 300 on the button. I hold A♠4♥ — a weak offsuit ace with a poor kicker. The pot is 1,050 and calling would cost me 400 more (700 total), which gives decent pot odds, but Claude’s 3‑bet range from the BB in this spot will be weighted toward hands that dominate my Ax (TT+/AK/AQ) and I’ll have difficult post‑flop decisions even with position. A minimum 4‑bet to 1,100 is too small to fold out meaningful parts of his range and would unnecessarily inflate the pot with a marginal hand. Given my preflop plan to avoid continuing vs large 3‑bets and the unfavorable equity/dominance situation, I’ll fold and wait for a better spot.

Tc2h is a very weak offsuit hand with poor high card strength and limited playability. Even on the button in a limped or small pot (pot 150, to-call 100 offers about 2.5:1), it has low equity against typical calling or limping ranges from blinds. Opponents like Minni and Claude have shown aggression in past hands, but this spot calls for folding trash to preserve stack and wait for premium hands or strong draws. Tight-aggressive strategy to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

Raising to 300 with AQ from the small blind. This is a premium hand that should be played aggressively. A 3x raise is standard pre-flop sizing and gives me good pot odds while building value. Minni is short-stacked, so applying pressure with a strong hand is the correct play. This also maintains a balanced raising range from the SB.

I'm in the big blind with K♦3♦ facing a 3x SB open to 300. K3 suited is marginal but has decent equity (king blocker + backdoor nut-flush) and the price to defend is cheap — I only need to call 200 more. I'm out of position postflop versus the SB, so I don't want to 3-bet light with this holding and get into tough spots; a min-raise to 500 would be a thin bluff. I'll defend the blind by calling the 200 additional chips (total commitment 300) to see a flop and then play cautiously: fold to heavy turn/river aggression unless I hit a pair/flush or a strong draw.

Checking on the flop with AQ against a paired board (JJ3). While I raised preflop with a premium hand, this particular texture doesn't warrant continuation betting. The paired board increases the likelihood Minni has a Jack in her range, and my AQ high is not strong enough to build the pot. Checking allows me to gather information, control pot size, and see the turn card while maintaining flexibility in my decision-making.

Flop is J♣ 3♠ J♠ and I hold K♦3♦ — I’ve flopped two pair (Jacks and Threes). That’s a strong but somewhat vulnerable hand (beats most of SB’s range except trips/sets or a full house). Claude was the preflop raiser and checked the flop, which can be either pot control or a slowplay. I want to extract value from weaker holdings and deny free cards that could turn into a full house for a lurking set, while keeping the pot manageable if I run into heavy resistance. I’ll make a value/protection bet of ~2/3 pot to charge weaker hands and probe the opponent: bet 400 into 600. If Claude check‑raises big I’ll strongly distrust my hand (likely trips/boat) and re‑evaluate (lean fold to a large check‑raise); if he just calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn.

Calling 400 to see the turn with AQ. The 3:1 pot odds are favorable against Minni's likely range (which probably doesn't include an actual Jack given the unusual board). She's short-stacked and likely applying pressure, potentially with weaker holdings or semi-bluffs. AQ retains strong equity on most turn cards. Position for the turn is valuable with two cards to come.

With a pair of Jacks and AKQ kickers on a board where Minni just checked after betting the flop, this is a premium spot to value bet. She's short-stacked with approximately 8185 chips and likely holds a broadway hand (AQ, KQ, KJ range) rather than a premium pair given her pre-flop call. Betting 1000 into the 1400 pot applies appropriate pressure, builds value from her weaker holdings, and maintains position advantage on the river. Even if she has a King, I retain substantial equity with Ace and Queen outs. This sizing is standard for turn value betting and keeps her in a decision that favors my hand strength.

I hold K♦3♦ and the turn K♣ gives me top two pair (Kings and Jacks). That’s a very strong showdown hand but it’s vulnerable to any Jack (trip/quads), pocket Kings or pocket Threes (full houses) and to many Kx hands with a better kicker. Claude’s line (check–call on the flop, now leading 1,000 on the turn) is polarized toward strong made hands or bluffs. Pot is 2,400 after his 1,000 — calling 1,000 requires ~29% equity. Given my hand strength, pot odds and stack sizes, I’m not folding; I’ll call the 1,000 to see the river and re-evaluate (fold to a very large raise/shove, extract value if he gives a normal river price).

With AQ high on a JJ3K8 board against an opponent who raised the flop and called my turn raise, my hand is weak and likely dominated. Checking preserves chips by avoiding additional losses when behind, shows down value as ace-high, and maintains a prudent short-handed strategy. Minni's range is heavily weighted toward holdings that beat me (Jx, Kx, broadway hands), making value betting or bluffing low-probability plays.

I hold Kd 3d on Jc 3s Js Kc 8c — two pair (Kings and Jacks). Claude’s line (preflop open, check‑call on the flop, then a sizable 1,000 bet on the turn that I called) is skewed toward strong made hands (Jx, Kx, pocket pairs) or a controlled value range. He checks the river, which is ambiguous, but many of the hands that will call a river value bet (any Jack/trips, most Kx with a kicker >3, KJ, full houses) beat me, while the hands I actually beat will often fold to a bet. Betting here is thin and likely negative EV; the safest +EV line is pot control and go to showdown. I check to avoid bloating the pot with a vulnerable two‑pair and will reassess if he fires a large river bet.

Pocket tens is a strong hand from the button in a 3-handed game. I'm in the best position and should play aggressively with this holding. A raise to 300 (3x the big blind) builds the pot and puts pressure on both opponents. This sizing is standard for raising premium pairs from late position and should either win the pot immediately or allow me to play a strong hand in position if called. With my 15,040 stack, this is a comfortable bet size that doesn't overcommit.

Claude opened to 300 on the button. I’m in the SB with 6♥6♣ and have 10,535 behind (~105bb). The pot is 450 and it costs me 250 more (300 total) to continue. 6-6 is a classic set-mining hand — with these effective stacks the implied odds are strong enough to justify a call rather than a small 3-bet out of position. A min-3-bet to 500 would commit me to playing a bigger pot OOP and open the door to a 4-bet; calling keeps the pot controlled and preserves the chance to hit a set. Plan: call to see the flop; if I flop a set or very favourable low/paired board I’ll play aggressively, otherwise proceed cautiously and fold to heavy action on high-card or coordinated boards.

K4o is a marginal hand at best with poor postflop playability, no suited connectors or strong drawing potential. Facing a button raise from Claude (wide range possible) and a SB call from Minni, the pot is multiway if I call, and I'm OOP. Pot odds are about 3.5:1 (200 to call into 700), but my equity is insufficient against their ranges, which include better kings, pairs, suited hands, etc. Standard fold to avoid tough postflop spots and preserve stack for premium opportunities in this deepstack tournament.

I called preflop with 6♣6♥ as a set-mining spot and missed the flop — board J♥ 9♠ 4♣ is relatively dry and I’m out of position versus the button. I don’t want to inflate the pot OOP with no set, so I’ll check and control the size. Plan: induce a c-bet and call small bets to realize set equity (call ~≤1/2 pot); fold to large overbets. If Claude checks back I’ll evaluate the turn and proceed only if I improve or get a clear reason to bluff.

I have pocket tens on a Jack-high flop. Minni checked after calling my preflop raise, which suggests weakness. This is a straightforward continuation bet situation. While the Jack on the board is concerning, my pocket pair likely has good equity. The flop is relatively dry without many draws, so betting 450 (roughly 65% of the pot) accomplishes several goals: (1) extracts value if Minni has a weak hand like a small pair or ace-high, (2) protects against any draw-heavy holdings, and (3) maintains my preflop aggression with a healthy stack. From the button in 3-handed play, controlling the pot is important. If Minni folds, I win a decent pot. If she calls, I'll have more information about her hand strength and can make a well-informed decision on later streets with my remaining 14,290 chips.

I checked the flop with 6♣6♥ intending to set-mine but missed this J♥9♠4♣ board and I’m out of position vs the button. Claude bets 450 into a pot that was ~700 (pot 1150 now); calling 450 to win 1600 requires ~28% equity. With no pair on the board and no backdoor flush/straight, my chance to hit a set by the river is only ~8.4%, far below the required equity. A raise as a bluff OOP would be costly and thin against the button’s c-bet range. Following my pre-flop plan to fold to heavy action on dry high-card boards, I’ll fold and preserve my ~102bb stack for better spots.

I’m on the button with Td8d and ~103 BB effective. In a 3-handed pot I should be opening a wider range — this hand has good playability (flush + straight potential) and benefits hugely from position. A standard 3x-ish sizing both builds the pot when I flop well and gives fold equity vs the blinds. Raise to 300 to seize initiative and either steal the blinds or play postflop in position. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll concede (fold) given hand strength; if called I’ll c-bet selectively on favorable flops and control the pot on heavy/connected boards.

T7o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability out of position in the small blind. Minni's button raise is likely a wide range given their aggressive history from prior hands, but defending this hand OOP risks difficult post-flop spots with dominated equity and limited fold equity if I 3-bet. Effective stacks are deep (142BB), so preserving stack for better spots is optimal in this multi-way potential pot against two opponents. Standard fold to avoid spew.

Minni raises from the button to 300, Elon folds from small blind. I'm in the big blind with 9s-2d, one of the weakest possible starting hands in poker. I need to call 200 more (450 total pot, needing 200 to call, giving me 2.25:1 pot odds).

Analysis:
- 9-2 offsuit is essentially unplayable. It ranks as one of the worst hands pre-flop
- Against Minni's button raise range, I have poor equity and very limited playability
- Even if I flop something with the 9 or 2, I'm unlikely to be ahead
- With a healthy stack of 15,340, there's no urgency to play marginal hands
- This is a straightforward fold to improve my overall decision quality

Folding weak hands from the big blind against aggressive late-position raises is solid fundamental poker strategy. I'll wait for better opportunities.

3-handed on the button with AJo offsuit, a strong premium hand with good playability and high card strength. Standard to raise for value and to take initiative, as folding or limping is suboptimal. Opponents are deep-stacked, and history shows frequent 300 raises preflop. Raising to 300 (3x BB) builds the pot while maintaining fold equity against blinds. Avoids limping which invites multiway pots with a non-speculative hand.

I'm holding 3c 2c in the small blind, which is the worst possible starting hand in poker. Elon raised to 300 from the button, and I need to call 250 more. With such a weak hand and no favorable odds, folding is the only reasonable action. Preserving my 15,290 chip stack for premium positions and better cards is essential at this stage of the tournament (hand 59/150). There's no value in playing 32o out of position against a raiser.

Elon opened to 300 from the button; I'm in the big blind with A♣T♥ and ~103bb. Calling the additional 200 (total commit 300) is the best play: ATo has good equity and post‑flop playability against a typical BTN opening range, and my immediate pot‑odds are favorable (I need roughly 31% equity to justify the call: 200/(450+200)). A min 3‑bet to 500 would give little fold equity and needlessly commit chips out of position — I don't have a strong enough hand to 3‑bet for value or to comfortably face a 4‑bet. Plan: see the flop and continue aggressively with top pair or strong draws; check/defend and control the pot when I miss.

I called preflop with A♣T♥ to see a flop and the board came 7♦ 2♠ 8♦. I missed a pair and have no diamond — just two overcards with limited backdoor potential. This is a fairly dry, low-impact flop and the BTN will c-bet a wide range. With deep stacks (≈100bb) I don’t need to bluff-bet here; checking controls the pot, lets me induce a c-bet, and preserves the option to float and realize my equity with two overcards/possible turn picks. Plan: check and call modest c-bets (roughly ≤ ~1/2 pot ≈ 300–350) to see a turn; fold or strongly re-evaluate against large overbets or check-raises unless I pick up a better read or improvement.

Preflop raise with AJo gives strong initiative on this flop of 7d2s8d, a relatively dry board with a weak flush draw (two diamonds) and no straight draws really. Minni's BB call range is wide, including suited connectors, Ax, pairs, but she often checks weak hands postflop. My AsJd has Ace-high equity, blocks AA/AK/AD, and Jd blocks some flush draws like JdX. No made hand, but position allows c-bet for value against weaker Ax/pairs/draws and fold equity against air/floats. Pot is 650; betting ~60% pot (400) maintains pressure without overbetting, balances with occasional value hands like 88/77/A8. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or letting her rep strength. Deep stacks favor aggressive play to build pot when ahead in equity/range advantage.

Call 400. I have A♣T♥ on 7♦‑2♠‑8♦: no pair and no diamond, but two overcards and an Ace blocker.
