A raise to the 500 minimum would commit me out of position with limited fold equity and is unnecessary with this holding. Plan postflop: play cautiously — continue with strong pairs, two-pair/straights, or good draws; check and fold to heavy pressure on dry boards; fold to a sizable 3-bet. Calling keeps the pot controllable and lets me realize equity out of the blind.

Checking with T9 on Q-2-Q board. This is a drawing hand (gutshot straight + pair outs) that needs to improve. With two opponents and a three-way pot, it's prudent to keep the pot cheap and see what opponents do rather than commit chips aggressively with a speculative hand. Pot control with a weak draw is the right approach here.

Board is Q♦ 2♦ Q♠; I have 8♣7♥ — no pair and no meaningful draw (only backdoor possibilities). Pot is 900 and Claude (SB) checked; BTN still to act. This is a dry, paired flop and I have very little equity or fold equity multiway, so betting out is a high-variance bluff that can be raised or called by any Q, pocket pair, or overpair. I’ll check to control the pot and see what the button does; plan is to fold to sizable continuation bets/raises and only continue if I improve or get a very cheap price to see the turn.

Flop is Qd 2d Qs, paired queens with diamond flush draw possible. I have 4h 3s, complete air with no pair, draw, or blockers to strong hands. Pre-flop I raised wide from button as a steal, which opponents called from blinds (Claude OOP SB call, Minni BB defend). Both checked, showing weakness or traps, but my wide range continuation bets often here for fold equity. Few Qx in my range (only QJ+, QA suited maybe), so high bluff frequency balances value like AA/KK/sets/AQ. Position excellent; if called, re-eval turn. Pot 900, bet 2/3 pot (600) standard size, risks small % stack (6575 deep effective), high fold equity vs calling ranges that often miss (e.g., suited connectors, mid pairs). Checking gives up initiative and lets them free card draws/improve; betting maximizes EV long-term.

Raising to 1500 to apply pressure on the short-stacked Elon and represent strength on the paired board. With T♥9♣, I have a gutshot straight draw plus two overcards, giving me legitimate equity. The key leverage is Elon's 5975 stack - my raise at 1500 total (900 more from my perspective) creates significant fold equity while my hand has outs if called. Minni's flop check suggests weakness, making this a good opportunity to steal the pot or narrow to heads-up against the short stack. This is more effective than calling and playing weakly post-flop.

I check-fold. Preflop I called with 8♣7♥ to see a flop, but the flop (Q♦ 2♦ Q♠) is extremely bad for my hand — no pair, no diamond, no useful draw. Elon c-bets 600 and Claude check-raises to 1500, putting 1500 into a 3000 pot; calling would require ~33% equity (1500 / (3000+1500)). My hand has essentially zero realistic equity (only remote runner-runner two‑pair/backdoor possibilities), far below the required threshold. Claude's large check-raise polarizes to very strong hands (Qx trips/full house, 22/set, possibly a diamond slowplay), and raising as a bluff into two players is high variance and unlikely to succeed. Folding preserves a healthy stack and avoids committing chips with no chance to win at showdown.

Facing a check-raise from Claude after my standard continuation bet on a Qd2dQs flop. My hand 4h3s is complete air with no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw, and poor blockers. Pre-flop I raised wide from button, but Claude's SB check-raise repolarizes the range towards strong value like QQ (trips), Qx combos, sets, or strong draws like nut diamond flush draws (AdXd, KdXd). Minni's fold doesn't change much. Pot is 3000, facing 900 to call (~25% pot), but even if I call, turn doesn't improve my hand much, and I'm OOP post-turn with nothing. Deep stacks (mine 5975 effective), but folding preserves chips for better spots. No fold equity on a raise here; raising would be suicidal vs his strong range. Standard fold to avoid spew.

7-6 offsuit is too weak to play from the button even with good position. The hand lacks connectivity and card strength. With a healthy stack and 70+ hands remaining, I should wait for better opportunities rather than speculate with marginal holdings.

Heads-up from the small blind with 5♦8♦ — a suited, connected-ish hand that plays well heads-up but is marginal out of position. Pot is 150 and it only costs 50 to call; min-raise is 200. Given the heads-up dynamics and Elon's tendency to defend wide, I want to take the initiative and apply pressure / pick up the pot preflop rather than passively limp and play OOP. I’ll raise to 300 (a standard 3x BB size at this table) to get fold equity and isolate. If called I’ll continuation-bet favorable flops (diamonds, low connected boards, or when I hit a pair) and control the pot on dry high-card boards or fold to heavy resistance. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and his tendencies.

Preflop in the big blind facing a standard small blind raise to 300 (200 to call into 400 pot). My hand 6d2h is one of the worst possible hands: offsuit, disconnected low cards with no high card value or suited potential. Pot odds are about 3:1 but the implied odds are terrible given my position out of position postflop and the hand's poor playability. No reason to speculate; folding trash preserves stack for better spots. Minni's stack is deep but doesn't change the fundamentals.

Button vs only the blinds with A♣6♥ — good spot to open. With three players left I should widen my range: A6o has useful blocker value and plays well in position. A standard 3xBB open (to 300) maximizes fold equity, helps isolate the SB, and keeps the pot a comfortable size versus Claude’s deep stack. If I'm called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on Ace-high boards (weak kicker). I’ll fold to large 3-bets/shoves from the shorter SB; vs small 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and reads.

My hand is 6s3h, a complete trash hand with no high card value, no suitedness for flush potential, and poor connectivity for straights. Facing a button raise from Minni to 300 into a 450 pot, requiring a 250 call from the small blind out of position. Pot odds are about 1.8:1, but this hand has negligible equity against Minni's likely wide button raising range (which includes many speculative hands but dominated mine). Postflop, I'll be OOP with minimal playability, high chance of folding to aggression. Stacks are deep (mine 58bb, others deeper), no need to speculate with the worst possible holding. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Prioritizing patience to maximize stack over 150 hands.

Folding J2o (Jack-Deuce offsuit) from the big blind against Minni's button raise. This is one of the weakest unpaired hands in poker with virtually no connectivity. Even though I'm getting 2.25:1 pot odds on my 200-chip call, J2o lacks the equity to profitably defend out of position against an aggressive button raiser. My J-high would struggle significantly post-flop with poor kicker, and I'm unlikely to have the best hand. With a reasonable stack (19,980) and many hands remaining (71 left), it's prudent to preserve chips and wait for stronger holdings where I have better positional or hand strength advantages.

93o is a complete trash hand with no high card value, connectivity, or suitedness. Poor playability postflop. Facing a preflop bet of 100 into a 150 pot (likely a raise from the blinds), folding is mandatory to preserve stack. Even on the button, this hand has zero equity against a calling range or better. No reason to speculate with 58BB effective stack when better spots will arise.

Folding 2-5o in the small blind against Minni's big blind. This is the worst hand in poker with minimal equity, and despite heads-up play typically requiring wider ranges, 2-5o is still too weak to justify calling. Minni will outplay this hand post-flop, and the discipline to fold bottom-tier hands contributes to long-term stack growth over 150 hands.

Raising to 250 total chips (1.5x normal raise) from the button with position advantage. Elon is shortstacked at 5,725 chips and likely to fold frequently. While As2h is weak, button position with a vulnerable opponent makes this an exploitative aggression. Recent play shows success with button raises against this dynamic. The raise is sized to win the pot preflop while not overcommitting with a marginal hand.

I’m in the small blind with K♦Q♣ facing a button open to 250 (pot 400). To call I need to put a total of 250 (200 more). KQo is strong enough to defend here — out of position vs the button I prefer to call and see a flop rather than make a thin, small 3-bet (min-raise to 400) which won’t buy the pot and a larger 3-bet would commit more and could draw a 4-bet from the BB (Elon, ~57bb). Calling keeps my range wide, avoids bloating a multiway pot unnecessarily, and lets me play postflop for position if the BB folds or proceed cautiously if it’s a 3-way pot. Plan: continue aggressively when I pair a king/queen or hit strong draws; fold/trim losses to significant aggression with missed flops out of position.

95 suited is a speculative hand with some playability due to suited connector potential for straights and flushes, and we get excellent pot odds (150 to win 600, requiring ~20% equity). However, facing a button raise from Claude (loose-aggressive based on history) and a small blind call from Minni (often calls wide but indicates some strength), multiway pots reduce equity significantly. Postflop position is decent (acting second), but 95s flops strong hands infrequently (gutshots, weak draws). With my shortest stack at 57BB, folding preserves chips for premium spots and avoids spewing in a bloated pot. Standard fold here to exploit better opportunities.

Flop is 8h Ac Ah and I have KdQc — I completely missed. This board is very bad for my hand: any Ace in Claude’s opening range gives him trips and will crush any single-pair improvement I can make. I’m out of position and have no draw or backdoor equity worth building a pot with. Best line is pot control: check and see what Claude does. If he checks back I can reassess on the turn; if he bets, I’ll fold to normal c-bet sizes (I’m only willing to entertain continuing to an extremely tiny probe bet, otherwise this is a fold).

Betting 400 on the flop with trips aces. Minni checked, indicating weakness or cautious play. This bet extracts value from her likely holding of AK/AQ/AT/A9 while keeping her engaged. The sizing (roughly 2/3 pot) is standard for value betting a premium hand out of position and maintaining pot control. With such a strong holding, I want to build value without overbet sizing that might fold out everything.

I checked the flop and Claude bet 400 into a 1000 pot on A♣ A♥ 8♥. I hold K♦ Q♣ with no pair and no meaningful draws. Calling 400 to win 1400 requires ~28.6% equity; my chance to hit a K or Q by the river is only ~24% (turn only ~12.8%). The board heavily favors a button opener’s range (lots of A-x, sets, and paired hands), I’m out of position, and this bet size is a standard c-bet that I should fold to. Preserving my ~14k stack and waiting for a better spot is the correct play here.

Button with Qh7s in a 3-handed game — pot is only the blinds (150) and it’s cheap to take the initiative. In 3-handed play I should be opening a much wider range from the button: Q7o is marginal but playable in position and has blocker value (queen). Raising applies pressure to the SB and BB, gives me fold equity, and lets me play postflop with position. The table’s common open sizing has been ~300, so I’ll make a standard open to 300 (3x BB) — big enough to discourage easy calls and multiway pots, small enough to keep postflop maneuverability. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet (I don’t want to bloat a pot out of position with Q7o), but c-bet favorable flops and exploit position if called. Calling 100 is too passive here and allows comfortable defense by the blinds, so I prefer to raise and take the lead.

JTo is a decent speculative hand with straight potential, overcards, and some showdown value. Minni's button raise is likely wide based on her frequent preflop aggression in previous hands (many raises from various positions). From the SB, defending wide against button opens is standard due to good pot odds: calling an additional 250 (total 300) into a growing pot (~700 after my call before BB acts) offers ~2.8:1 direct odds plus strong implied odds given deep stacks (56BB effective). Out of position but manageable postflop with this hand's playability. Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with a marginal hand against potential BB squeeze. Prefer calling to realize equity multiway or isolate if BB folds.

K-3 suited is a marginal hand even with the discount to call. While I'm getting 3.5:1 odds on my 200 chip call, K-3 is weak even in position. Minni's light button raise can be exploited later with premium holdings. My stack is healthy (20,180), so I don't need to play marginal hands. Elon's short stack (5,425) makes this a spot where better opportunities will present themselves. Discipline on hand selection is crucial in the mid-stages of a 150-hand tournament. I'll preserve chips and wait for higher equity spots.

Preflop call with JTo was for speculative value and pot odds, but this flop (A-8-6 rainbow) completely misses my holding: no pair, no draws (only gutshot to the 9 for a inferior straight), jack-high showdown value is minimal against Minni's button raising range which includes many Ax, pairs, and suited connectors that connect here. OOP first to act in a 700-chip pot, betting has poor fold equity against her positionally advantaged range; she'd continue wide with ace-high or better. Checking is mandatory to pot control and realize any thin equity if she checks behind weak hands, but prepared to fold to a bet as she c-bets frequently postflop per hand history (e.g., aggressive continuation in pots like #13, #34). No value, minimal bluff incentive this deep (54BB) on coordinated board where she can float/call/raise.

I opened preflop and Elon just checked the A♣ 8♦ 6♠ flop. With Q♥7♠ I have no pair and no clean draw (only running backdoors), so this is a marginal hand. Betting here would be a pure bluff a lot of the time versus a SB caller-heavy range (Ax, pocket pairs, broadways) who will often call or raise; I don’t want to bloat a pot out of position against lines that dominate me. In position I prefer pot control and to realize equity — check and see how he reacts. Plan: check to keep the pot small, fold to a large bet, and only continue to modest bets/calls where I can still fold cheaply or have reasonable odds to improve.

Turn board pairs the 6s, creating trips potential and filling some straight draws, but JTo still completely misses with jack-high and no draws (gutshot to 9 is speculative and inferior). Preflop call was for equity realization and pot odds, but this runout provides no improvement. OOP first to act in 700 pot vs Minni's button range (wide due to her frequent aggression, e.g., many raises in hands #10-12, #24, etc.), which caps at Ax, pairs, suited connectors that often hit this board (Aces, 87s, 76s, etc.). She checked flop behind, showing some weakness (possible air, floats, or traps), but betting now has no value and limited fold equity deep (~50BB effective) on a paired board where she defends wide positionally. Checking preserves stack, controls pot, induces bluffs on river if she has showdown value, or sees free card. Folding to bets remains optimal. Consistent with avoiding spew in marginal spots to grind long-term stack growth.

I opened preflop but on the turn I have Q♥7♠ against a board A♣ 8♦ 6♠ 6♥ with no pair and no real draws. Both players checked flop and Elon checked the turn — betting now would be a pure bluff that will often be called by any Ax or a pocket pair. I want to keep the pot under control in position and realize my equity; I’ll check the turn, see the river, and only continue to modest river bets or call small bluffs. If the river pairs my Q or 7 I can re-evaluate; I’ll fold to a large river bet but can call smaller bluffs.

River Jack pairs my Jack, giving me top pair with a Ten kicker on a board of A♣-8♦-6♠-6♥-J♠. Preflop call with JTo was speculative, and while flop/turn missed, river massively improves to a strong hand. Minni's button raise preflop is wide (per history of frequent aggression), but her checking behind flop (Ace-high rainbow) and turn (pairs low 6s) suggests capping range without top pair/Ace or strong draws: likely weaker pairs (77-55, suited connectors missing), floats, or air that she couldn't bet profitably OOP to my range. Ace likely bets flop for value/protection; trips/sets overbet earlier. Deep stacks (~50BB effective) and two checks behind indicate showdown value but also room for thin value extraction from her positionally wide calling range (8x, 97, lower pairs). Betting 400 (~57% pot) targets value from worse pairs/draws while denying equity/setting up fold equity if she has pure air. Checking risks free showdown (she may have 8x/Jx) or missed value; bet exploits her history of calling post-oak (e.g., #9, #63) without overcommitting stack. Optimal for stack maximization.

I opened wide preflop and exercised pot control on the flop/turn with Q♥7♠. The river bet is 400 into a 1,100 pot, so calling 400 risks 400 to win 1,500 — I need ~26.7% equity to justify a call. Elon's line (call preflop, check-check on A‑8‑6, then a small river stab on J) is much more consistent with thin value hands (A‑x, Jx, maybe an 8 or medium pair) than with a large bluff frequency — there were almost no realistic draws for him to miss and turn into a bluff. Q7 has virtually no showdown value and is dominated by the bulk of his value range, so I don't have the required equity or blocker coverage to make a speculative call. Preserving my deep stack and waiting for a better spot is the correct EV play here.

Pre-flop on the button in a 3-handed pot with 82o offsuit, a very weak hand with poor equity, playability, and high card strength. Standard play is to fold weak hands like this first in, even 3-handed, to preserve stack for stronger spots. Opponents are deep-stacked and aggressive based on history, but no need to enter pot with trash. Goal is long-term stack maximization over 150 hands, so avoid spew.

Folding 5-7 offsuit from the small blind. While getting 3:1 pot odds on the additional 50 chips, this hand is too weak to play against an aware opponent in Minni. With a healthy 20K+ stack and 67 hands remaining, I can afford to be selective and preserve chips by folding marginal holdings. The 100 chips already invested (blind) shouldn't influence the decision on future action.

Raising A3o from the Button in 3-handed play. In short-handed poker, button ranges expand significantly. With a solid stack and position, raising is standard here. This puts pressure on the blinds and wins the pot preflop a good portion of the time. If called, I play in position with an Ace and fold equity.

Facing a button open to 300 from the small blind with 9♣6♣ and stacks ~137/198/60 BB. Pot is 450 and I need to put in 250 more (total committed = 300). 9-6 suited is a speculative hand with good postflop equity and nut-flush/straight potential — worth seeing a flop multiway with deep stacks. A min 3-bet to 500 is a weak, positionally awkward play OOP that risks bloating the pot and getting 4-bet or called by better hands; folding is too tight given the price and implied odds. Plan is to call, see the flop, and proceed only with pair/draws or strong textures; fold to heavy aggression on dry, unfavorable boards.

King-eight offsuit is a marginal hand at best, especially out of position in the big blind facing a button raise from Claude (who has been aggressive) and a call from Minni. The pot lays about 3.5:1, but post-flop playability is poor multiway OOP with no strong draws or suitedness. Folding preserves my stack for better opportunities in this long tournament.

I called preflop as a speculative, deep-stack play with 9♣6♣, but the flop Q♦-8♦-3♥ gives me nothing — no pair, no diamond draw and only a very remote backdoor club flush. I’m out of position vs the button raiser (Claude) who c-bets a wide range here, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a weak holding. Plan: exercise pot control and see how he acts — check and fold to meaningful pressure. If he checks back I can take a free turn card and reassess, but I’ll give up to a bet.

I raised preflop from the button with A3o, which is a standard play in 3-handed play. Minni called from the small blind, and the flop came 8d-Qd-3h. I've made a pair of 3s with an Ace kicker. 

Minni checked, which is consistent with either a weak hand or a slowplay. Given the board texture (two diamonds, two broadway cards), this is an excellent spot to continuation bet:

1. **Fold equity**: My preflop raise gives me fold equity, especially since many hands Minni might have called with (like small pairs, weak aces, or broadway cards) will struggle against my range here.

2. **Board texture**: The Q and 8 are good scare cards, and the diamond draws are out there. A bet pressures draws and weak holdings.

3. **Position**: I'm in position, which is crucial for extracting value and controlling the pot.

4. **Hand strength**: While A3 is not premium, I have a made hand (pair of 3s) with good kicker.

5. **Stack depth**: With 19830 chips, I can comfortably continue this line.

I'll bet 250 (roughly 36% of the pot), which is a standard continuation bet sizing that charges draws and weak hands while keeping the pot manageable.

I called preflop as a speculative, deep-stack play with 9♣6♣ to see a flop, but the Q♦-8♦-3♥ flop misses me completely — no pair, no diamond draw and only a remote backdoor club. I'm out of position versus Claude (button) and he continuation-bets 250 into a ~950 pot. Even though the bet is small, I have virtually no equity and poor turn/river outs to continue; calling to float OOP is a weak, losing line and will just commit more chips if he applies pressure. Per my preflop plan (check and fold to meaningful pressure), I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Button with A♠5♦ — an ace with position and an ace-blocker makes this a good stealing candidate. Pot is 150, to call 100; min-raise is only 200 but that small sizing invites multiway calls and squeezes. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x BB) to take the initiative, isolate the SB, and capitalize on positional advantage. Plan: fold to a substantial 3-bet from Claude (As5o plays poorly as a 4-bet bluff candidate against deep stacks), but if called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and realize my equity. Calling 100 is too passive here and misses value/pressure opportunities.

Preflop as SB facing button raise to 3BB from Minni, who appears aggressive and raises frequently from late position based on hand history. My 4h7h is suited with straight potential (gutshots, wheels). OOP but deep effective stacks (~60BB), good implied odds if I hit a draw or flush. Pot odds favorable: calling 250 into ~700 pot post-call. SB defend range vs BTN open includes low suited connectors/gappers like 76s+, 54s+, and 47s fits marginally. Folding too tight OOP; 3-bet not ideal with this hand. Call to realize equity and play postflop.

Calling with Ad5h from big blind. Getting 3.5:1 odds to see a flop. The ace gives me solid equity and potential to make top pair. While out of position, the pot odds justify playing this hand. Plan to evaluate the flop carefully and play the ace aggressively if it hits while being cautious with just the five-kicker.

Flop of 7d-9s-9h gives me bottom pair (7s) with a strong heart flush draw (9h on board plus my two hearts), plus backdoor straight potential. This is a monster hand multiway OOP with huge equity against Minni's button range (wide, draw-heavy) and Claude's BB calling range. Pot is 900, my stack 5725 effective. Leading out protects my hand, denies equity to draws (straights like 8T, 68, 8x flush draws), and extracts value from worse pairs/draws/one-pair hands. Bet sizing 2/3 pot (600) balances value and protection without overbetting into trips possible ranges. Checking risks free cards to villainous draws or letting them check back weak hands.

Calling with Ad5h on a paired board (7d 9s 9h). While out of position and likely behind Elon's raising range, I'm getting 2.5:1 pot odds. I have a strong kicker in the Ace and wheel draw outs (any 8, 6, 4, 3, or 2 improves me significantly). Pot odds and implied odds justify continuing here rather than folding a hand with upside potential. This is a conditional call rather than a raise due to poor positioning and the strength Elon's bet demonstrates.

Flop: 7♦ 9♠ 9♥. Elon bets 600 and Claude calls — pot is 2,100 and I need ~22% equity to continue (600/(2100+600)). I raised preflop so I have position and an Ace-blocker (As) which reduces some strong A9/A7 combos, but A5 has no pair and very little fold equity raising into two players. I’ll call 600 to realize equity, keep the pot manageable, see the turn, and re-evaluate: if I improve (pair/ace) I can value; if checked to me on a blank I can consider a delayed c‑bet; I’ll fold to significant turn/river aggression without improvement.

Turn 7c pairs the sevens on the board, improving my hand to trips (7h with 7d/7c) while keeping it ahead of many one-pair hands, floats, missed draws (spade flush draws, OESD like T8/J8/Tx), and weaker pairs from callers' ranges. Opponents called my flop bet on a paired 79r board multiway, indicating medium strength: likely 9x (trips 9s, which beats me), 97 (boat), but also overpairs that missed, flush draws (hearts/spades/diamonds), gutshots. Minni (aggressive BTN raiser pre) and Claude (loose BB caller) can have wide calling ranges including these. Pot 2700, OOP first to act; betting 2/3 pot (1800) gets value from worse (Ax flush draws, 8x, QJ etc.), protects vs river draws (river could bring 8/T for straights, heart/spade flush), denies equity, and balances my range after leading flop. Check risks free cards/check-back of Ax/weak 9x, or facing raise from nuts. With 5125 stack (effective ~80% pot), sizing leaves room for river. Shoving too thin, small bet risks min-raise jam from boats.

Folding Ad5h on the turn.

Current board (7d 9s 9h 7c) is double-paired, which significantly limits my hand's potential. I have ace-high with no made hand and no meaningful draws:
- The turn card (7c) was a blank that didn't improve my wheel draw possibilities

Elon's aggressive turn raise to 1800 from a small stack (3325 total) suggests strength - likely a made pair or better. This is a clean fold that preserves chips for better situations later in the tournament.

Turn: board 7♦ 9♠ 9♥ 7♣, Elon bets 1,800 into a 4,500 pot and Claude folds — now heads‑up. Elon led the flop and now barrels a large turn bet into a double‑paired board. That line and sizing heavily favor a full house (he likely had a 9 on the flop or a 7 that now becomes a boat). Calling 1,800 to win ~6,300 requires ~28.6% equity; A♠5♦ has virtually no clean outs on this runout. Raising would be spewy — committing more into a likely value range with almost no fold equity. 3-handed on the button with Q8o, excellent position. Q8o is a standard opening hand here with good playability, straight potential, and queen blocker. Blinds are passive based on history (frequent limps, calls, check-downs). Pot is 150, to-call 100 gives great implied odds if called. Raising steals dead money or builds pot in position. Size to 300 (3x BB) is standard, minraise too small. Folds too weak in position, limping plays poorly OOP postflop. High EV play to maximize stack long-term.

7-3 offsuit is statistically the worst hand in poker. While I have good stack depth at 19,580 chips, there's no reason to get involved with this hand out of position against Elon's button raise. The hand has negligible equity, minimal playability post-flop, and I'm still facing potential action from Minni in the big blind. With 150 hands to play, I should preserve my chips for premium hands and favorable spots rather than bleeding chips in difficult situations with weak holdings.

Call to 300. Pot is 450 and I need to call 200, so break-even equity is ~200/(450+200) ≈ 31%. 9♣8♥ has good heads-up playability and reasonable equity vs a button opening range, so calling to see a flop is the best low-risk line. I’m out of position so I don’t want to 3-bet and bloat the pot with a marginal hand; calling preserves stack and gives implied odds to realize straights/pairs. Postflop plan: evaluate the texture — if I hit a pair or a strong draw I’ll continue aggressively; if I miss and face heavy aggression I’ll fold to avoid costly OOP play.

Heads-up on Ah 4c Qs with 9c8h — a complete miss. I have no pair, no meaningful backdoor flush, and very little equity versus a button opener’s c-bet range (lots of Ax, Qx and broadways that connect well here). If Elon bets I’ll fold to standard continuation bet sizes; if he checks back I get a free turn card and can re-evaluate with a chance to realize implied odds.

3-handed button vs BB caller on Ah4cQs flop. Elon opened preflop and is now continuing with a 400 bet on Ah 4c Qs. I have 9c8h — a complete miss: no pair, no realistic flush draw (only one heart on board + my 8h, impossible to make a five-card heart flush with two cards to come) and only 6 clean outs to pair (9s,9d,9h,8c,8d,8s). I'm on the button with J9o, a marginal but playable hand from late position. Key considerations:

5. Action: Raising to 300 applies pressure on the blinds, is reasonable sizing (3x BB), and:

   - If called, I maintain position and can control the action postflop
   - Keeps my aggressive table image strong

This is standard button play in a healthy chip position against weakened opponents.

I have A♠K♠ in the small blind facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I only need 250 more to call, but calling keeps it multiway and plays me out of position. 5s3h is a complete trash hand with minimal playability, especially suited but low and disconnected. Calling 700 more (1000 total) with Jh9s from the button. I can control the action on the flop and exploit my positional advantage. 4-betting would overvalue this hand; calling allows me to win the pot through intelligent postflop play.

I 3-bet preflop to isolate and am now heads-up on a very dry flop (2c 4s 5h). I hold A♠K♠ — two big overcards and a backdoor spade possibility. Calling 1,050 with Jh9s on a 2c4s5h flop. Position is maintained through the turn, equity is approximately breakeven with pot odds, and implied odds are favorable if I hit an overcard. The dry flop texture means many of my 10 outs are clean. I 3‑bet preflop with A♠K♠ to isolate and c‑bet the dry 2♣‑4♠‑5♥ flop for value/pressure; Claude called. The turn J♣ is a blank for my hand — I didn’t pair and my backdoor spade-runner is dead. Betting 2800 on the turn with J♥9♠ on 2♣4♠5♥J♣. The Jack significantly improved my hand to top pair with a 9 kicker, which is very strong on this dry board. I 3‑bet preflop with A♠K♠ to isolate and c‑bet the very dry 2♣‑4♠‑5♥ flop. I checked the J♣ on the turn to control the pot and planned to fold to large aggression. Raising as a bluff would bloat a pot I’m unlikely to win and is too risky given stack depths. Therefore I fold and preserve chips for better spots.

Button with Q♠9♦ — a hand I want to play in position. With only two opponents and deep stacks, I should open rather than limp; raising takes the initiative, builds fold equity, and lets me play post‑flop from the button. I’ll size standard (~3x BB) to 300: big enough to discourage wholesale defense but small relative to our stack. Plan: proceed aggressively in position if called; fold (or seriously reevaluate) to a large 3‑bet from Claude out of position.

Preflop in SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB) with K9o. This is a marginal offsuit king-high hand with no strong blockers or suited potential. With 80BB effective stacks and tournament goal to maximize long-term, folding preserves stack for better spots. Historical aggression from opponents noted, but discipline key.

I'm in the big blind with KJ, a premium broadway hand. Minni raised to 300 from the button, and Elon folded. This puts me in a heads-up situation.

This is a standard call that allows me to proceed to the flop and leverage my positional advantage and chip lead. KJ plays well against a wide range of hands.

Betting 400 on the flop with top pair top kicker (K♣J♥ on J♣6♠3♣). This is roughly 60% of the current 650 chip pot and represents a standard value bet/c-bet. The bet size is aggressive enough to apply pressure on her short stack while remaining reasonable for value purposes.

Call 400. Q♠9♦ missed the J♣6♠3♣ flop and I have no flush/straight draw, but Claude (BB) called my preflop raise so his defending range is wide. His bet is small into ~1050, so the call requires only ~28% equity (400/(1050+400) ≈ 27.6%). In position I can float and apply pressure or fold to genuine strength on the turn — this preserves fold equity and keeps the pot manageable. A raise here with no pair/draw would be too ambitious and often face a call or reraise from better hands. Plan: call to see a turn and continue only if the turn helps (Q/9 or a favorable runout) or he shows weakness; fold to big turn aggression.

Turn value bet with TPKO (K♣J♥ on J♣6♠3♣8♦). The neutral 8 on the turn doesn't significantly improve Minni's calling range. With a substantial chip advantage (21,030 vs 9,495) and her short stack, I should continue applying pressure while building value. A bet of 800 (roughly 55% pot) is standard - strong enough to fold out air/weak hands while extracting value from draws and weaker pairs. This protects my hand equity and maintains aggression.

I floated the flop with Q♠9♦ but the turn 8♦ is a blank for my hand. Claude’s bet of 800 into a ~2250 pot requires ~26% equity to justify a call (800/(2250+800) ≈ 26%). My clean outs by the river are roughly 10 cards (3 queens + 3 nines + 4 tens for the 8-9-10-J-Q straight) → ~10/46 ≈ 21.7% equity, which is below the pot‑odds threshold. Claude’s line (call preflop, flop c-bet, turn bet) looks value-heavy (Jx, 8x, sets or made clubs) and a raise here would be an ambitious bluff likely to be called or reraised. I’ll conserve chips for better spots and fold.

Preflop on the button with Ks4h (offsuit King-high). Position is excellent, allowing control postflop. Hand has King blocker, decent high card value, and potential to make top pair. Blinds are unopened (pot 150 from blinds), so standard to open-raise wide from button to steal or isolate. Limping or calling is weak OOP postflop; folding misses steal equity. +EV spot in tournament to build stack.

3d-Jc is a weak, marginal hand that doesn't justify continuing in the small blind against a button raise. While pot odds (450:250) are reasonable in isolation, the key factors against calling are: (1) the hand is easily dominated post-flop, (2) if the big blind folds, I'm out of position heads-up with a weak hand against Elon's aggressive button range, and (3) even if Minni calls, my hand plays poorly multi-way. My stack is healthy at 22,430, allowing me to be selective and wait for higher-quality spots. I’m in the big blind with A♠T♦ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, to call 200). Heads-up ATo is a very playable hand — often ahead of the button’s opening range (lots of Kx, Qx, suited broadways and connectors). I want initiative and to isolate Elon rather than seeing a flop out of position and playing passively. A small 3-bet (to 500) gives him good odds to continue; a standard 3-bet to ~3x the open is cleaner. I’ll 3-bet to 900 total: large enough to fold out weaker hands and take the lead postflop, but small enough to keep hands that I dominate in. If he 4-bets very large or jams, I’ll fold ATo; if he calls I’ll c-bet favorable textures and apply pressure.

Preflop on button facing a 3-bet to 900 from Minni in BB after my open to 300 (pot 1250, to call 600). Ks4h is a weak offsuit King-high with poor postflop playability and bad kicker for top pair. Minni's frequent preflop aggression suggests a polarized 3-bet range (value like QQ+/AK or bluffs), but K4o has poor equity (~25-35%) against either and folds to 3-bets from button opens in standard play. Calling puts me OOP postflop with a marginal hand lacking strong draws or domination potential. 4-betting risks isolation OOP with a weak ace-high blocker hand against Minni's 86BB stack, low fold equity given history. Stacks are deep (77BB effective), so folding preserves stack for +EV spots without committing 8% of stack to a marginal situation. Discipline key in tournament to avoid spew.

T-5o is too weak to play even on the button. While position is valuable, it doesn't compensate for the poor hand quality and limited equity. Both opponents are short-stacked and defensive, making this a weak spot to get creative. Better to preserve chips for stronger holdings where I can leverage my positional and stack advantages more effectively.

Holding 7♣3♠ in the small blind is one of the worst starting hands — very low equity and no playability. The button folded, so I’m heads-up against the big blind, out of position postflop. I can call 50 to ‘complete’ or raise to 200, but calling puts me OOP with a hand that rarely improves and raising with this trash is a poor-seeded bluff that will often get me 3-bet or called and dominated. With a healthy stack and plenty of better spots to pick, the +EV line is to save chips and fold preflop.

Button with T5s and ~98BB effective: this is a clear open-raising spot. T5s is playable postflop (suited, straight/flush potential) and I want to seize position and fold equity rather than limp. I’ll make a standard 3xBB open to 300 to apply pressure to the blinds and keep ranges wide. Plan: if I face a big 3-bet I’ll fold this marginal holding; if called I’ll use positional advantage to c-bet selectively and realize equity on favorable boards.

Pocket sevens is a strong hand heads-up or 3-handed, especially against a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive with wide ranges in previous hands. As SB OOP, calling risks a squeeze from BB Claude and gives up initiative. 3-betting isolates the button, builds the pot with equity advantage, and applies pressure on Minni's wide range. Sizing to 900 (3x the raise) is standard, maintaining fold equity while keeping stacks deep post-flop (effective ~8k). Folding is too tight; flat is passive.

Folding K7o to a 3-bet. With a weak hand and facing aggression from both opponents, plus a strong chip lead, this is a straightforward fold. K7o doesn't have sufficient equity against reasonable 3-bet ranges and I don't need to take marginal spots this early in the tournament.

Elon 3-bet to 900 out of the small blind after my 300 button open. Calling 600 into a 1300 pot gives ~31.5% pot odds to continue; with T5s I have good post‑flop playability (spade backdoor + straight potential) and the huge advantage of position against a 3-bettor who is out of position. Effective stacks are ~68BB so I can realize equity and maneuver post‑flop. A thin 4-bet bluff here is unnecessary and risky; folding loses too much equity.
